papers.title: Towards Scalable One-Step Generative Modeling for Autoregressive Dynamical System Forecasting
papers.summary: Fast surrogate modeling for high-dimensional physical dynamics requires more than low short-term error: useful models must roll out efficiently while preserving the statistical structure of long trajectories. Neural operators provide inexpensive autoregressive forecasts but can drift in turbulent regimes, whereas rolling diffusion and latent generative surrogates can represent stochastic transitions at the cost of multi-step denoising, noise-schedule design, or auxiliary compression models. We propose MeanFlow Long-term Invariant Spatiotemporal Consistency Autoregressive Models (MeLISA), a latent-free autoregressive generative surrogate built on pixel-space MeanFlow. MeLISA defines a blockwise stochastic transition kernel that generates each forecast block with a single model evaluation, avoiding latent encoders and iterative diffusion solvers at inference time. To stabilize long-horizon rollouts, MeLISA combines a Window-Consistency MeanFlow objective that learns conditional spatiotemporal generation from partially observed temporal windows with a Time Increment Consistency loss that constrains multi-lag finite increments and targets temporal-correlation structure. We evaluate MeLISA with compact UNet and scalable DiT backbones on two high-resolution benchmarks, extended 2D Kolmogorov flow at $256 \times 256$ and turbulent channel-flow slice at $192 \times 192$. MeLISA outperforms neural-operator baselines on short-term forecasting accuracy and long-horizon statistical metrics, including energy spectra, turbulent kinetic energy, and mixing-rate-related dynamics, while achieving inference speeds comparable to, and in some cases faster than, neural operators. Compact 3.7-5.7M-parameter variants already deliver strong parameter efficiency, and DiT variants provide a scalable path up to 150M parameters. Overall, MeLISA benefits both rollout efficiency and long-horizon statistical accuracy.

ai.tldr: 提出MeLISA模型，一种基于像素空间MeanFlow的无潜在变量自回归生成代理，实现单步生成预测，避免迭代扩散，在湍流模拟中兼顾短期精度与长期统计一致性。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子长期滚动易漂移，滚动扩散模型需多步去噪且依赖潜在编码器，缺乏高效且统计一致的生成式代理。
ai.method: MeLISA采用块状随机转移核，通过窗口一致性MeanFlow目标学习部分观测窗口的条件时空生成，并加入时间增量一致性损失约束多步有限增量与时间相关结构。
ai.result: 在256×256 Kolmogorov流和192×192湍流通道切片上，MeLISA在短期预测精度和长期统计指标（能谱、湍动能、混合率）上超越神经算子基线，推理速度相当或更快，参数效率高。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展的单步生成式代理方法，适用于高维物理动力学的快速且统计一致的长期预测。

idea_spark.idea: 将MeLISA的块状生成策略与物理信息损失结合，进一步提升长期统计保真度。
idea_spark.risk: 块状生成可能引入边界伪影，需设计平滑过渡机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受扩散模型与神经算子局限启发，探索无潜在编码器的单步生成范式。

en.tldr: Proposes MeLISA, a latent-free autoregressive generative surrogate based on pixel-space MeanFlow, enabling one-step forecast generation without iterative diffusion, achieving both short-term accuracy and long-term statistical consistency in turbulent simulations.
en.motivation: Existing neural operators drift in long rollouts, while rolling diffusion models require multi-step denoising and latent encoders, lacking efficient and statistically consistent generative surrogates.
en.method: MeLISA uses a blockwise stochastic transition kernel, learns conditional spatiotemporal generation from partially observed windows via a Window-Consistency MeanFlow objective, and adds a Time Increment Consistency loss to constrain multi-lag finite increments and temporal correlation structure.
en.result: On 256×256 Kolmogorov flow and 192×192 turbulent channel flow, MeLISA outperforms neural-operator baselines in short-term accuracy and long-term statistical metrics (energy spectra, TKE, mixing rate), with comparable or faster inference and strong parameter efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable one-step generative surrogate for fast and statistically consistent long-term forecasting of high-dimensional physical dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Combine MeLISA's blockwise generation with physics-informed losses to further improve long-term statistical fidelity.
idea_spark.risk: Blockwise generation may introduce boundary artifacts, requiring smooth transition mechanisms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by limitations of diffusion models and neural operators, exploring latent-free one-step generative paradigms.

papers.title: Adaptive Coordinate Transforms for Neural Operators
papers.summary: Neural operators have achieved promising performance on partial differential equations (PDEs), but most existing models are built on fixed Eulerian coordinates. This mismatch between evolving physical structures and static coordinates creates spatial misalignment, leading to unnecessarily non-local operator mappings and reinforcing a smoothness preference near sharp transitions. Inspired by adaptive coordinate transformations in classical PDE analysis, we propose the Adaptive Coordinate Transform (ACT) block, a plug-and-play module for data-driven geometric adaptation in neural operators. ACT blocks resolve this structural limitation by learning adaptive coordinate systems within the operator learning pipeline. Specifically, given an input feature, the ACT block learns a coordinate transformation and represents the same feature under the transformed coordinates via differentiable sampling. This operation preserves the underlying signal while changing its spatial representation, equivalent to expressing the same physical quantity in different coordinate systems. By adapting the coordinate system to the data, ACT allows the network to better track evolving structures, reduce operator complexity, and dynamically focus on critical features to improve learning. We evaluate the proposed approach across diverse PDE benchmarks and multiple neural operator architectures. Experimental results demonstrate consistent and significant improvements in predictive accuracy, indicating that learning coordinate systems provides a powerful mechanism for enhancing operator learning.

ai.tldr: 提出自适应坐标变换模块，解决神经算子在固定欧拉坐标下处理偏微分方程时的空间错位问题。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子基于固定欧拉坐标，与演化物理结构不匹配，导致空间错位、非局部映射和锐利过渡平滑化。
ai.method: 设计ACT模块，通过学习坐标变换并用可微采样重表示特征，实现数据驱动的几何自适应。
ai.result: 在多个PDE基准和神经算子架构上，预测精度一致且显著提升。
ai.help_to_user: 提供即插即用的坐标自适应模块，可直接增强现有神经算子的性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将坐标变换学习与神经算子结合，动态聚焦关键特征，降低算子复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 可微采样可能引入插值误差，且坐标变换的优化可能增加训练不稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经典PDE分析中自适应坐标变换启发，将物理直觉融入深度学习架构。

en.tldr: Proposes Adaptive Coordinate Transform blocks to resolve spatial misalignment in neural operators for PDEs by learning data-driven coordinate systems.
en.motivation: Existing neural operators use fixed Eulerian coordinates, causing spatial misalignment with evolving physical structures, non-local mappings, and over-smoothing near sharp transitions.
en.method: Introduces ACT blocks that learn coordinate transformations and represent features under transformed coordinates via differentiable sampling, enabling geometric adaptation.
en.result: Consistent and significant improvements in predictive accuracy across diverse PDE benchmarks and neural operator architectures.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play module to enhance any neural operator with adaptive coordinate learning.

idea_spark.idea: Combine coordinate transformation learning with neural operators to dynamically focus on critical features and reduce operator complexity.
idea_spark.risk: Differentiable sampling may introduce interpolation errors, and optimizing coordinate transforms could increase training instability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by adaptive coordinate transformations in classical PDE analysis, bridging physical intuition with deep learning architectures.

papers.title: Physical Fidelity Reconstruction via Improved Consistency-Distilled Flow Matching for Dynamical Systems
papers.summary: Reconstructing high-fidelity flow fields from low-fidelity observations is a central problem in scientific machine learning, yet recent diffusion and flow-matching models typically rely on iterative sampling, making them costly for latency-sensitive workflows such as ensemble forecasting, real-time visualization, and simulation-in-the-loop inference. We study whether a high-fidelity flow-matching generative model can be compressed into a compact one-step model for fast scientific flow reconstruction. Our approach distills an optimal-transport flow-matching teacher into a one-step consistency model. Low-fidelity observations are incorporated at inference by initializing the generative trajectory from a noised observation along the transport path, allowing an unconditional high-fidelity flow model to perform conditional reconstruction without retraining the teacher. We evaluate this distillation strategy on three fluid benchmarks, Smoke Buoyancy, Turbulent Channel Flow, and Kolmogorov Flow, using coarse-to-fine reconstruction as a controlled testbed at field sizes up to $256 \times 256$. Across these settings, the distilled student retains similar performance of the teacher's model on spectrum metrics, while using roughly half as many parameters and achieving a $12\times$ inference speedup over the flow-matching teacher. Under the same training budget, the distilled student also outperforms a one-step consistency model trained directly from scratch by $23.1\%$ in SSIM, showing that teacher distillation improves training efficiency rather than merely accelerating sampling. These results suggest a promising route for turning future high-capacity scientific generative models into compact reconstruction models that are faster to train, cheaper to run, and easier to deploy.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种改进的一致性蒸馏流匹配方法，将高保真流匹配生成模型压缩为单步模型，用于从低保真观测中快速重建流场，在三个流体基准上实现12倍推理加速且性能相当。
ai.motivation: 现有扩散和流匹配模型依赖迭代采样，计算成本高，难以用于集合预报、实时可视化等延迟敏感的科学工作流。
ai.method: 将最优传输流匹配教师模型蒸馏为单步一致性模型，推理时通过沿传输路径对噪声观测初始化生成轨迹，实现无条件的教师模型进行条件重建。
ai.result: 蒸馏学生模型在谱指标上与教师性能相当，参数量减半，推理速度提升12倍；在相同训练预算下，SSIM比从头训练的一致性模型高23.1%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种将高容量科学生成模型转化为紧凑、快速、易部署的重建模型的通用路线。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将蒸馏策略与物理约束结合，在单步推理中同时保证数据一致性和物理守恒律。
idea_spark.risk: 蒸馏可能损失极端事件或小尺度结构的重建精度，需在特定应用中验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受一致性模型加速扩散模型采样的启发，将其引入科学流匹配领域。

en.tldr: We propose an improved consistency-distilled flow matching method that compresses a high-fidelity flow-matching generative model into a one-step model for fast flow field reconstruction from low-fidelity observations, achieving 12× speedup with comparable performance on three fluid benchmarks.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion and flow-matching models rely on iterative sampling, which is computationally expensive for latency-sensitive scientific workflows such as ensemble forecasting and real-time visualization.
en.method: We distill an optimal-transport flow-matching teacher into a one-step consistency model. At inference, low-fidelity observations are incorporated by initializing the generative trajectory from a noised observation along the transport path, enabling conditional reconstruction without retraining the teacher.
en.result: The distilled student retains similar spectral performance as the teacher, uses roughly half the parameters, and achieves 12× inference speedup. Under the same training budget, it outperforms a one-step consistency model trained from scratch by 23.1% in SSIM.
en.help_to_user: This provides a general route to turn high-capacity scientific generative models into compact, fast, and easy-to-deploy reconstruction models.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining distillation with physics constraints to enforce both data consistency and physical conservation laws in a single step.
idea_spark.risk: Distillation may degrade reconstruction of extreme events or small-scale structures, requiring validation in specific applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by consistency models that accelerate diffusion sampling, we adapt this idea to scientific flow matching.

papers.title: A Robust Foundation Model for Conservation Laws: Injecting Context into Flux Neural Operators via Recurrent Vision Transformers
papers.summary: We propose an architecture that augments the Flux Neural Operator (Flux NO), which combines the classical finite volume method (FVM) with neural operators, with ViT-based context injection. Our model is formulated as a hypernetwork: it extracts solution dynamics over a finite temporal window, encodes them with a recurrent Vision Transformer, and generates the parameters of a context-conditioned neural operator. This enables the model to infer and solve conservation laws without explicit access to the governing equation or PDE coefficients. Experimentally, we show that the proposed method preserves the robustness, generalization ability, and long-time prediction advantages of Flux NO over standard neural operators, while delivering reliable numerical solutions across a broad range of conservative systems, including previously unseen fluxes. Our code is available at https://github.com/xx257xx/CONTEXT_FLUX_NO.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合循环视觉Transformer的Flux神经算子架构，通过超网络注入上下文信息，无需显式方程即可求解守恒律。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子在未知通量或系数时泛化能力不足，需要一种能隐式推断守恒律的鲁棒模型。
ai.method: 使用循环视觉Transformer编码有限时间窗口内的解动态，生成条件神经算子的参数，实现上下文注入。
ai.result: 该方法保持了Flux NO的鲁棒性、泛化能力和长期预测优势，在多种保守系统（包括未见通量）上提供可靠数值解。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将视觉Transformer与神经算子结合，以处理未知守恒律方程。

idea_spark.idea: 用循环视觉Transformer替代传统编码器，从解轨迹中隐式学习物理参数。
idea_spark.risk: 超网络参数生成可能增加计算开销，且对长时窗依赖敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将有限体积法的离散思想与Transformer的序列建模能力融合，启发新的混合数值方法。

en.tldr: Proposes a Flux Neural Operator augmented with a recurrent Vision Transformer for context injection, enabling robust conservation law solving without explicit PDE coefficients.
en.motivation: Standard neural operators lack generalization to unknown fluxes or coefficients; a robust model that infers conservation laws implicitly is needed.
en.method: A hypernetwork uses a recurrent Vision Transformer to encode solution dynamics over a finite temporal window and generate parameters for a context-conditioned neural operator.
en.result: The method preserves the robustness, generalization, and long-time prediction advantages of Flux NO, delivering reliable solutions across diverse conservative systems, including unseen fluxes.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to combine Vision Transformers with neural operators for solving unknown conservation laws.

idea_spark.idea: Replace traditional encoders with a recurrent Vision Transformer to implicitly learn physical parameters from solution trajectories.
idea_spark.risk: Hypernetwork parameter generation may increase computational cost and be sensitive to long time windows.
idea_spark.inspiration: Fusing finite volume discretization with Transformer sequence modeling inspires new hybrid numerical methods.

papers.title: Reduced-Order Modeling of Parameterized Visco-Plastic Shallow Flows
papers.summary: We propose a non-intrusive reduced-order modeling framework for parametrized visco-plastic free-surface flows governed by a shallow-water formulation of Herschel--Bulkley fluids. These flows exhibit strong nonlinearities, non-smooth rheology, moving fronts, and yield surfaces, making efficient surrogate modeling particularly challenging. To address this challenge, we employ a tensor-based approach in which the solution manifold is approximated using a low-rank representation obtained via higher-order singular value decomposition of snapshot data over a structured parameter space.   The resulting tensorial reduced-order model (TROM) enables rapid online evaluation by directly reconstructing solution trajectories from the compressed representation, thereby avoiding the need to perform time integration of a reduced dynamical system. The proposed non-intrusive framework can be interpreted as an encoder--decoder architecture with a compressed latent representation and efficient multilinear decoding. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach accurately captures key flow features, including front propagation, plug and shear regions, and near-stopping dynamics, while achieving substantial computational speedups relative to full-order simulations.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种非侵入式降阶建模框架，用于参数化粘塑性浅水流动，通过张量方法实现快速在线评估。
ai.motivation: 粘塑性自由表面流动具有强非线性、非光滑流变、移动前沿和屈服面，传统降阶建模面临挑战。
ai.method: 采用基于张量的方法，通过高阶奇异值分解对结构化参数空间上的快照数据进行低秩表示，构建张量降阶模型，避免时间积分。
ai.result: 数值实验表明，该方法准确捕捉了前沿传播、塞流和剪切区域以及近停止动力学，并实现了显著的计算加速。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高效模拟复杂粘塑性浅水流动的降阶建模技术，适用于参数化研究。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将张量降阶模型与深度学习结合，以处理更复杂的参数空间和非结构化网格。
idea_spark.risk: 张量分解对参数空间的规则性要求较高，非结构化参数空间可能降低效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受编码器-解码器架构启发，将降阶模型视为压缩表示与多线性解码的组合。

en.tldr: A non-intrusive reduced-order modeling framework for parameterized visco-plastic shallow flows using tensor methods for rapid online evaluation.
en.motivation: Visco-plastic free-surface flows exhibit strong nonlinearities, non-smooth rheology, moving fronts, and yield surfaces, making efficient surrogate modeling challenging.
en.method: A tensor-based approach approximates the solution manifold via low-rank representation from higher-order singular value decomposition of snapshot data over a structured parameter space, avoiding time integration.
en.result: Numerical experiments show accurate capture of front propagation, plug and shear regions, and near-stopping dynamics with substantial computational speedups.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient reduced-order modeling technique for simulating complex visco-plastic shallow flows in parameterized studies.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining tensor reduced-order models with deep learning to handle more complex parameter spaces and unstructured grids.
idea_spark.risk: Tensor decomposition requires structured parameter spaces; unstructured spaces may reduce efficiency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by encoder-decoder architectures, viewing reduced-order models as compressed latent representations with multilinear decoding.

papers.title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks with Learnable Loss Balancing and Transfer Learning
papers.summary: We propose a self-supervised physics-informed neural network (PINN) framework that adaptively balances physics-based and data-driven supervision for scientific machine learning under data scarcity. Unlike prior PINNs that rely on fixed or heuristic weighting of physics residuals and data loss, our approach introduces a learnable blending neuron that dynamically adjusts the relative contribution of each term based on their uncertainties. This mechanism enables stable training and improved generalization without manual tuning. To further enhance efficiency, we integrate a transfer learning strategy that reuses representations from related domains and adapts them to new physical systems with limited data. We validate the framework for the prediction of heat transfer in liquid-metal miniature heat sinks using only 87 CFD datapoints, where the adaptive PINN achieves an error <8%, outperforming shallow neural networks, kernel methods, and physics-only baselines. Our framework provides a general recipe for embedding physics adaptively into neural networks, offering a robust and reproducible approach for data-scarce problems across various scientific domains, including fluid dynamics and material modeling.

ai.tldr: 提出一种自适应平衡物理和数据损失的可学习PINN框架，结合迁移学习，在仅87个CFD数据点上实现<8%的误差。
ai.motivation: 现有PINN依赖固定或启发式权重平衡物理残差和数据损失，手动调参困难且泛化性差。
ai.method: 引入可学习混合神经元动态调整损失权重，并采用迁移学习复用相关领域表示。
ai.result: 在液态金属微型散热器热传导预测中，自适应PINN误差<8%，优于浅层网络、核方法和纯物理基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需手动调参、鲁棒且可复现的物理嵌入神经网络方法，适用于数据稀缺的科学问题。

papers.title: Meta-learning for sample-efficient Bayesian optimisation of fed-batch processes
papers.summary: The optimisation of fed-batch (bio)chemical process recipes is subject to inherent, underlying, and unmeasurable fluctuations across batches, whose trajectories are difficult to model and costly to measure. Bayesian Optimisation (BayesOpt) is a powerful tool for sampling and optimisation of expensive-to-measure functions. Gaussian Processes (GPs), the surrogate models used in BayesOpt, are static, forecast poorly, and lack generalisation across experiments, limiting their applicability to time-varying batch processes with stochastic parameters, i.e., process fluctuations. This work investigates System-Aware Neural ODE Processes (SANODEP) as a meta-learning model to overcome the limitations of GPs and increase few-shot optimisation performance in BayesOpt. Using a penicillin batch production case study, we find that SANODEP outperforms GP-based BayesOpt in the low-data regime, resulting in improved objectives when few experimental runs are performed. These improvements are observed in both on- and off-distribution batches, highlighting the generalisation capabilities of SANODEP. Using this approach, batch process operators can accelerate the initial optimisation steps in BayesOpt by deploying meta-learning or optimise the process with fewer experiments when the experimental cost is high.

ai.tldr: 提出使用系统感知神经ODE过程（SANODEP）作为元学习模型，替代高斯过程，在贝叶斯优化中实现补料分批过程的少样本高效优化。
ai.motivation: 补料分批过程存在批次间不可测波动，高斯过程模型静态、预测差且缺乏泛化能力，难以有效优化。
ai.method: 将SANODEP作为元学习代理模型嵌入贝叶斯优化框架，利用其泛化能力处理时变随机参数。
ai.result: 在青霉素分批生产案例中，SANODEP在低数据场景下优于基于高斯过程的贝叶斯优化，在分布内和分布外批次上均提升目标值。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助批次过程操作员在实验成本高时，通过元学习加速初始优化步骤或减少所需实验次数。

idea_spark.idea: 将SANODEP与主动学习结合，动态选择最具信息量的实验点以进一步降低数据需求。
idea_spark.risk: 元学习模型对训练分布外场景的泛化能力可能有限，需验证其鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受神经过程在少样本学习中的成功启发，将其应用于过程优化以克服高斯过程的静态局限。

en.tldr: Proposes System-Aware Neural ODE Processes (SANODEP) as a meta-learning model to replace Gaussian Processes for sample-efficient Bayesian optimisation of fed-batch processes.
en.motivation: Fed-batch processes suffer from unmeasurable batch-to-batch fluctuations; Gaussian Processes are static, poor at forecasting, and lack generalisation across experiments.
en.method: Integrates SANODEP as a meta-learning surrogate model within Bayesian optimisation to handle time-varying stochastic parameters.
en.result: In a penicillin batch production case study, SANODEP outperforms GP-based BayesOpt in low-data regimes, improving objectives on both on- and off-distribution batches.
en.help_to_user: Enables batch process operators to accelerate initial optimisation steps via meta-learning or optimise with fewer experiments when experimental cost is high.

idea_spark.idea: Combine SANODEP with active learning to dynamically select the most informative experimental points for further data efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Meta-learning models may have limited generalisation to out-of-distribution scenarios; robustness needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of neural processes in few-shot learning, applied here to overcome the static limitations of Gaussian Processes in process optimisation.

papers.title: Active Learning for Communication Structure Optimization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
papers.summary: Optimizing the communication structure of large language model based multi-agent systems (LLM-MAS) has been shown to improve downstream performance and reduce token usage. Existing methods typically rely on randomly sampled training tasks. However, tasks may differ substantially in difficulty and domain, and thus they are not equally informative for updating communication structure, making optimization under limited training budgets often unstable and highly sensitive to the particular training set. To actively identify the most valuable tasks for communication-structure optimization, we propose an ensemble-based information-theoretic task selection framework. The proposed method estimates task informativeness by how much a candidate task changes the distribution over graph parameters, using ensemble Kalman inversion as an efficient and derivative-free approximation of the corresponding Bayesian update. The resulting estimator is especially suitable for black-box and noisy multi-agent systems. To enhance scalability, we construct a compact candidate pool through embedding-based representative selection and combine the informative selection with surrogate modeling and batch Thompson sampling. We validate our method in both benign settings and settings with agent attacks, demonstrating its effectiveness for communication-structure optimization under constrained computational budgets.

ai.tldr: 提出基于集成卡尔曼反演的信息论任务选择框架，主动识别最有价值的任务以优化LLM多智能体系统的通信结构，在有限预算下提升性能并减少token消耗。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖随机采样训练任务，但任务难度和领域差异大，导致通信结构优化不稳定且对训练集敏感。
ai.method: 利用集成卡尔曼反演近似贝叶斯更新，估计候选任务对图参数分布的影响程度，结合嵌入代表性选择和批量汤普森采样提高可扩展性。
ai.result: 在良性设置和智能体攻击设置下均验证了有效性，能在有限计算预算下优化通信结构。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过主动学习选择最有效的训练任务来优化多智能体系统的通信拓扑，节省计算资源。

idea_spark.idea: 将集成卡尔曼反演与主动学习结合，为图结构优化提供无导数、高效的贝叶斯更新近似。
idea_spark.risk: 候选池构建和批量采样可能引入偏差，需验证在不同任务分布下的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将信息论度量与贝叶斯方法用于其他复杂系统的结构学习，如网络科学或机器人协作。

en.tldr: Proposes an ensemble-based information-theoretic task selection framework using ensemble Kalman inversion to actively identify the most valuable tasks for optimizing communication structure in LLM-based multi-agent systems, improving performance and reducing token usage under limited budgets.
en.motivation: Existing methods rely on randomly sampled training tasks, but tasks vary in difficulty and domain, making optimization unstable and sensitive to the training set.

idea_spark.risk: Candidate pool construction and batch sampling may introduce bias; robustness across different task distributions needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires using information-theoretic measures and Bayesian methods for structure learning in other complex systems, such as network science or robot collaboration.

papers.title: Von Neumann Networks
papers.summary: In the mid-twentieth century, mathematician and polymath John von Neumann created a computational system on an array of cells as a simple model of the human brain, where each cell had one of a finite set of roles or states that he predicted would be modelled by a diffusion process. In this work, we show that such a system, when developed in a modern deep learning setting, enables the construction of an artificial neuron having specialized roles that can be learnt. We refer to this neuron as the Von Neumann neuron, and the resulting neural network from such neurons result in a self-engineered design whose architecture is only dependent on the structure and locations of its inputs and outputs on this cellular array. The mathematical framework for these Von Neumann Networks (VNNs) is also constructed and shows that they are based on the extension of neural operators and the learning of Green's functions with convolutions on a cellular topology having a diffusion signature. We also prove that these VNNs are part of a more general computational system called Cellular Machines that are computationally universal. Initial experiments show that VNN based multi-layered perceptrons outperform their equivalent deep learning variant on basic tasks, while being more parameter efficient and are capable of learning new types of tasks. This includes the ability to solve for and construct an extension of the Von Neumann (hardware) architecture common to all modern computers to cells and suggests new opportunities that could be explored.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于冯·诺依曼细胞阵列的新型神经网络结构，其神经元角色可学习，架构由输入输出位置自动决定，并证明其计算通用性。
ai.motivation: 受冯·诺依曼早期细胞计算模型启发，探索将扩散过程与深度学习结合，构建自设计、参数高效的神经网络。
ai.method: 定义冯·诺依曼神经元，在细胞阵列上通过卷积学习格林函数，扩展神经算子，形成冯·诺依曼网络（VNN）。
ai.result: VNN多层感知机在基础任务上优于传统深度学习变体，参数更少，能学习新任务，并扩展至细胞级冯·诺依曼架构。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种自组织、计算通用的神经网络设计思路，可能降低对人工架构设计的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 利用扩散过程自动生成网络连接，替代手工设计，实现架构与任务匹配。
idea_spark.risk: 大规模细胞阵列的计算开销和训练稳定性尚需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将经典计算模型（细胞自动机）与现代深度学习算子结合，开辟自设计网络新方向。

en.tldr: Proposes a novel neural network based on von Neumann's cellular array, where neuron roles are learnable and architecture is determined by input/output locations, with proven computational universality.
en.motivation: Inspired by von Neumann's early cellular computation model, explores combining diffusion processes with deep learning to build self-designed, parameter-efficient neural networks.
en.method: Defines von Neumann neurons, learns Green's functions via convolutions on a cellular topology, extending neural operators to form Von Neumann Networks (VNNs).
en.result: VNN-based MLPs outperform traditional deep learning variants on basic tasks with fewer parameters, learn new task types, and extend von Neumann architecture to cells.
en.help_to_user: Offers a self-organizing, computationally universal neural network design that may reduce reliance on manual architecture engineering.

idea_spark.idea: Use diffusion processes to automatically generate network connections, replacing handcrafted designs for task-adaptive architectures.
idea_spark.risk: Computational cost and training stability at scale remain to be validated.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining classical cellular automata with modern neural operators opens new directions for self-designing networks.

papers.title: AeroJEPA: Learning Semantic Latent Representations for Scalable 3D Aerodynamic Field Modeling
papers.summary: Aerodynamic surrogate models are increasingly used to replace repeated high-fidelity CFD evaluations in many-query design settings, but current approaches still face two important limitations: they often scale poorly to the very large fields arising in realistic 3D aerodynamics, and they rarely produce latent representations that are directly useful for analysis and design. We introduce AeroJEPA, a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for aerodynamic field modeling that addresses both issues. Rather than predicting the full flow field directly from geometry, AeroJEPA predicts a target latent representation of the flow from a context latent representation of the geometry and operating conditions, and optionally reconstructs the field through a continuous implicit decoder. This formulation decouples latent prediction from field resolution while encouraging the latent space to organize semantically. We evaluate AeroJEPA on two complementary datasets: HiLiftAeroML, which stresses the method in a high-fidelity regime with extremely large boundary-layer fields, and SuperWing, which tests large-scale generalization and latent-space optimization over a broad family of transonic wings. Across these benchmarks, AeroJEPA is competitive as a continuous surrogate for aerodynamic fields, scales naturally to high-resolution outputs, and learns context and predicted latents that encode geometry and aerodynamic quantities not used directly as supervision. We further show that the resulting latent space supports controlled interpolation, linear probing, concept-vector arithmetic, and a constrained design latent-optimization experiment. These results suggest that predictive latent learning is a promising direction for scalable and design-meaningful aerodynamic surrogate modeling.

ai.tldr: 提出AeroJEPA，一种联合嵌入预测架构，用于可扩展的三维气动场建模，学习语义潜表示以支持分析和设计。
ai.motivation: 现有气动代理模型难以扩展到真实三维大尺度流场，且潜表示缺乏直接用于分析和设计的语义信息。
ai.method: 从几何和工况的上下文潜表示预测流场的目标潜表示，并通过连续隐式解码器可选重建流场，解耦潜预测与场分辨率。
ai.result: 在HiLiftAeroML和SuperWing数据集上，AeroJEPA在连续代理、高分辨率输出和潜空间语义组织方面具有竞争力，支持可控插值、线性探测、概念向量算术和约束设计优化。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展且设计有意义的气动代理建模方法，潜表示可直接用于分析和优化。

idea_spark.idea: 利用潜空间语义组织实现跨工况的零样本或小样本预测。
idea_spark.risk: 潜表示可能过度依赖训练数据分布，泛化到极端几何或工况时性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将潜预测与连续解码解耦的思路可启发其他高分辨率物理场代理设计。

en.tldr: AeroJEPA is a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for scalable 3D aerodynamic field modeling that learns semantic latent representations useful for analysis and design.
en.motivation: Existing aerodynamic surrogates scale poorly to large realistic 3D fields and produce latent representations lacking direct utility for analysis and design.
en.method: Predicts a target latent representation of the flow from a context latent representation of geometry and operating conditions, with optional field reconstruction via a continuous implicit decoder, decoupling latent prediction from field resolution.
en.result: On HiLiftAeroML and SuperWing datasets, AeroJEPA achieves competitive continuous surrogate performance, scales to high-resolution outputs, and learns semantically organized latents supporting controlled interpolation, linear probing, concept-vector arithmetic, and constrained design optimization.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable and design-meaningful aerodynamic surrogate modeling approach with latents directly usable for analysis and optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage semantic latent organization for zero-shot or few-shot prediction across different operating conditions.
idea_spark.risk: Latent representations may overfit to training data distribution, degrading performance on extreme geometries or conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: The decoupling of latent prediction from continuous decoding inspires surrogate designs for other high-resolution physical fields.

papers.title: BRICKS: Compositional Neural Markov Kernels for Zero-Shot Radiation-Matter Simulation
papers.summary: We introduce a new strategy for compositional neural surrogates for radiation-matter interactions, a key task spanning domains from particle physics through nuclear and space engineering to medical physics. Exploiting the locality and the Markov nature of particle interactions, we create a \emph{next-particle prediction} kernel using hybrid discrete-continuous transformer models based on Riemannian Flow Matching on product manifolds. The model generates variable-sized typed sets of particles and radiation side effects that are the result of the interaction of an incident particle with a material volume. The resulting kernel can be composed to simulate unseen large-scale material distributions in a zero-shot manner. Unlike mechanistic simulators, our model is designed to be differentiable, provides tractable likelihoods for future downstream applications. A significant computational speed-up on GPU compared to CPU-bound mechanistic simulation is observed for single-kernel execution. We evaluate the model at the kernel level and demonstrate predictive stability over multi-round autoregressive rollouts. We additionally release a novel 20M-event radiation-matter interaction dataset for further research.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于组合式神经马尔可夫核的零样本辐射-物质模拟方法，利用局部性和马尔可夫性质，通过混合离散-连续Transformer模型和黎曼流匹配生成粒子与辐射副作用集，实现可微分、可组合的快速模拟。
ai.motivation: 传统机械模拟器计算慢、不可微分，难以用于大规模材料分布和下游任务。
ai.method: 构建“下一粒子预测”核，采用混合离散-连续Transformer模型，基于乘积流形上的黎曼流匹配，生成变长类型化粒子集。
ai.result: 单核执行在GPU上比CPU机械模拟显著加速；多轮自回归展开中预测稳定；发布了2000万事件数据集。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速模拟辐射与任意材料组合的相互作用，支持可微分和零样本泛化。

idea_spark.idea: 利用黎曼流匹配在乘积流形上生成离散-连续混合输出，实现可微分模拟。
idea_spark.risk: 模型在极端材料分布或罕见粒子类型上可能泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受粒子相互作用的局部性和马尔可夫性质启发，将模拟分解为可组合的核。

en.tldr: Introduces compositional neural Markov kernels for zero-shot radiation-matter simulation, leveraging locality and Markov property with hybrid discrete-continuous transformers and Riemannian Flow Matching on product manifolds to generate variable-sized typed particle sets, enabling differentiable and composable fast simulation.
en.motivation: Traditional mechanistic simulators are slow, non-differentiable, and unsuitable for large-scale material distributions and downstream tasks.
en.method: Builds a next-particle prediction kernel using hybrid discrete-continuous transformer models based on Riemannian Flow Matching on product manifolds, generating variable-sized typed sets of particles and radiation side effects.
en.result: Single-kernel execution achieves significant GPU speedup over CPU-bound mechanistic simulation; predictive stability over multi-round autoregressive rollouts; releases a 20M-event dataset.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast, differentiable, zero-shot simulation of radiation interactions with arbitrary material compositions.

idea_spark.idea: Uses Riemannian Flow Matching on product manifolds to generate discrete-continuous mixed outputs for differentiable simulation.
idea_spark.risk: May generalize poorly to extreme material distributions or rare particle types.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the locality and Markov nature of particle interactions, decomposing simulation into composable kernels.

papers.title: Sequential Design of Genetic Circuits Under Uncertainty With Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: The design of biological systems is hindered by uncertainty arising from both intrinsic stochasticity of biomolecular reactions and variability across laboratory or experimental conditions. In this work, we present a sequential framework to optimize genetic circuits under both forms of uncertainty. By employing simulator models based on differential equations or Markov jump processes alongside a reinforcement learning (RL) policy-based approach, our method suggests experiments that adapt to unknown laboratory conditions while accounting for inherent stochasticity. While previous Bayesian methods address uncertainty through iterative experiment-inference-optimization cycles, they typically require computationally expensive inference and optimization steps after each experimental round, leading to delays. To overcome this bottleneck, we propose an amortized approach trained up-front across a distribution of possible uncertain parameters. This strategy sidesteps the need for explicit parameter inference during the design cycle, enabling immediate, observation-based adaptation. We demonstrate our framework on models for heterologous gene expression and a repressilator circuit, showing that it efficiently handles both molecular noise and cross-laboratory variability.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于强化学习的顺序框架，在分子随机性和实验条件变异双重不确定性下优化基因电路设计。
ai.motivation: 生物系统设计受内在随机性和实验室条件变异影响，现有贝叶斯方法迭代计算昂贵、延迟高。
ai.method: 使用微分方程或马尔可夫跳跃过程模拟器，结合强化学习策略，预先在参数分布上训练，实现基于观测的即时适应。
ai.result: 在异源基因表达和抑制子电路模型上验证，有效处理分子噪声和跨实验室变异。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何用强化学习高效处理基因电路设计中的不确定性，避免传统贝叶斯方法的计算瓶颈。

idea_spark.idea: 将强化学习与模拟器结合，实现无需显式参数推断的在线自适应设计。
idea_spark.risk: 预先训练可能无法覆盖所有实际参数分布，需注意泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将强化学习用于其他实验设计场景，如蛋白质工程或代谢通路优化。

en.tldr: A sequential framework using reinforcement learning to optimize genetic circuits under both intrinsic stochasticity and cross-laboratory variability.
en.motivation: Biological system design is hindered by uncertainty from molecular noise and lab conditions; previous Bayesian methods are computationally expensive and slow.
en.method: Uses differential equation or Markov jump process simulators with a reinforcement learning policy, pre-trained over a distribution of uncertain parameters for immediate observation-based adaptation.
en.result: Demonstrated on heterologous gene expression and repressilator models, efficiently handling molecular noise and cross-lab variability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how reinforcement learning can efficiently handle uncertainty in genetic circuit design, avoiding the computational bottleneck of Bayesian methods.

idea_spark.idea: Combine reinforcement learning with simulators for online adaptive design without explicit parameter inference.
idea_spark.risk: Pre-training may not cover all real-world parameter distributions; generalization needs attention.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying reinforcement learning to other experimental design tasks, such as protein engineering or metabolic pathway optimization.

papers.title: Do Neural Operators Forget Geometry? The Forgetting Hypothesis in Deep Operator Learning
papers.summary: Neural operators perform well on structured domains, yet their behaviour on irregular geometries remains poorly understood. We show that this limitation is not merely an encoding issue, but a depth-wise failure mode inherent to deep operator architectures.   We formalise the Geometric Forgetting Hypothesis: due to the Markovian structure of operator layers and their reliance on global mixing mechanisms, neural operators progressively lose access to domain geometry as depth increases. Using layer-wise geometric probing, we demonstrate that both spectral and attention-based operators systematically lose geometric fidelity.   We show that this geometric forgetting degrades accuracy, stability, and generalisation. To counteract it, we introduce a lightweight geometry memory injection mechanism that restores geometric constraints at intermediate depths with minimal architectural overhead. This simple intervention consistently mitigates forgetting and exposes a geometric shortcut instability in transformer-based operators, revealing that geometric retention is a structural requirement rather than a design choice.

ai.tldr: 神经算子在非规则几何上表现不佳，本文提出几何遗忘假说，并通过层间几何探测验证，引入轻量几何记忆注入机制有效缓解遗忘。
ai.motivation: 神经算子在结构化域表现良好，但在不规则几何上行为不明，现有研究认为仅是编码问题，本文揭示其本质是深度架构的逐层失效模式。
ai.method: 形式化几何遗忘假说，通过层间几何探测分析谱算子和注意力算子，提出轻量几何记忆注入机制在中间层恢复几何约束。
ai.result: 几何遗忘降低精度、稳定性和泛化性；几何记忆注入有效缓解遗忘，并暴露基于Transformer算子的几何捷径不稳定性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解神经算子在不规则几何上的失效机制，并提供一种轻量改进方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在训练过程中动态调整几何记忆注入的强度，或结合自适应几何注意力机制。
idea_spark.risk: 几何记忆注入可能增加计算开销，且对高度不规则几何的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类视觉系统对几何信息的层级保留机制启发，将几何约束作为结构需求而非设计选择。

en.tldr: Neural operators fail on irregular geometries due to depth-wise geometric forgetting; a lightweight geometry memory injection mitigates this.
en.motivation: Neural operators perform well on structured domains but poorly on irregular geometries; this is not just an encoding issue but a depth-wise failure mode.
en.method: Formalize the Geometric Forgetting Hypothesis, use layer-wise geometric probing on spectral and attention-based operators, and introduce a lightweight geometry memory injection mechanism.
en.result: Geometric forgetting degrades accuracy, stability, and generalization; geometry memory injection effectively mitigates forgetting and reveals geometric shortcut instability in transformer-based operators.
en.help_to_user: Understand the failure mechanism of neural operators on irregular geometries and obtain a lightweight improvement method.

idea_spark.idea: Explore dynamically adjusting the strength of geometry memory injection during training or combining with adaptive geometric attention.
idea_spark.risk: Geometry memory injection may increase computational cost, and its generalization to highly irregular geometries needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the hierarchical retention of geometric information in the human visual system, treating geometric constraints as a structural requirement rather than a design choice.

papers.title: Universal Adaptive Proximal Gradient Methods via Gradient Mapping Accumulation
papers.summary: We propose an adaptive proximal gradient method for minimizing the sum of two functions, where one is a simple convex function, and the other belongs to one of the three classes: nonconvex smooth, convex nonsmooth, or convex smooth. The key feature of the method is an adaptive step size that accumulates historical gradient mapping norms in the denominator. Without any modification or knowledge of problem parameters, the method converges across all three problem classes under mild bounded-iterates and bounded-variance assumptions, with rates matching those of the proximal gradient method up to logarithmic factors, in both deterministic and stochastic settings. For the convex setting, we further propose an accelerated variant. It retains a similar near-optimal convergence rate for the nonsmooth case and achieves an improved rate of order $\widetilde{O}\big(1/t^2 + σ/\sqrt{t}\big)$ for the smooth case, which is optimal up to logarithmic factors. Notably, we develop new techniques for controlling the effect of stochastic noise, which are applicable across all three problem classes in the stochastic setting and enable simplified analysis.

ai.tldr: 提出一种通用自适应近端梯度方法，通过累积历史梯度映射范数自适应调整步长，在非凸光滑、凸非光滑和凸光滑三类问题上均达到近最优收敛率。
ai.motivation: 现有自适应近端梯度方法通常针对特定问题类设计，缺乏统一框架；且需要问题参数先验知识，限制了实用性。
ai.method: 在步长分母中累积历史梯度映射范数，无需修改或问题参数知识；并针对凸情形提出加速变体。
ai.result: 在确定性和随机设置下，三类问题的收敛率与近端梯度法匹配（仅差对数因子）；加速变体在凸光滑情形达到最优率 Õ(1/t² + σ/√t)。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的自适应优化器，适用于多种常见优化问题，无需手动调参。

idea_spark.idea: 将历史梯度映射范数作为步长分母，类似AdaGrad但针对复合目标函数设计。
idea_spark.risk: 累积项可能导致步长过早衰减，在非凸问题中可能陷入不良局部解。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从AdaGrad和近端梯度法的结合中受到启发，利用历史信息统一处理不同问题类。

idea_spark.risk: 累积项可能导致步长过早衰减，在非凸问题中可能陷入不良局部解。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从AdaGrad和近端梯度法的结合中受到启发，利用历史信息统一处理不同问题类。

en.tldr: Proposes a universal adaptive proximal gradient method that adapts step sizes by accumulating historical gradient mapping norms, achieving near-optimal convergence rates across nonconvex smooth, convex nonsmooth, and convex smooth problems.
en.motivation: Existing adaptive proximal gradient methods are often designed for specific problem classes and require prior knowledge of problem parameters, limiting their practicality.
en.method: Accumulates historical gradient mapping norms in the denominator of the step size without modification or parameter knowledge; an accelerated variant is also proposed for convex settings.
en.result: In both deterministic and stochastic settings, convergence rates match those of proximal gradient methods up to logarithmic factors; the accelerated variant achieves optimal rate Õ(1/t² + σ/√t) for convex smooth case.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play adaptive optimizer suitable for a wide range of common optimization problems without manual tuning.

idea_spark.idea: Use historical gradient mapping norms as the denominator of the step size, similar to AdaGrad but designed for composite objective functions.
idea_spark.risk: The accumulation term may cause premature step size decay, potentially leading to poor local minima in nonconvex problems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the combination of AdaGrad and proximal gradient methods, leveraging historical information to unify handling of different problem classes.

papers.title: RepFlow: Representation Enhanced Flow Matching for Causal Effect Estimation
papers.summary: Estimating causal effects from observational data has become increasingly critical in diverse fields including healthcare, economics, and social policy. The fundamental challenge in causal inference arises from the missing counterfactuals and the selection bias. Existing methods are largely limited to point estimates and lack the capacity for distribution modeling. In this work, we propose RepFlow, a novel framework that formulates causal effect estimation as a joint optimization problem integrating representation learning with Conditional Flow Matching (CFM).   RepFlow mitigates selection bias by minimizing the entropically regularized Wasserstein distance between treated and control representations.   To enhance numerical stability, we further introduce an $L_2$ normalization constraint on latent representations.   This balanced representation enables the flow model to accurately capture the distribution of potential outcomes. Extensive experiments across a wide range of benchmarks demonstrate that RepFlow consistently outperforms existing methods in both point and distributional causal effect estimation.

ai.tldr: 提出RepFlow框架，将表示学习与条件流匹配结合，用于因果效应估计，解决缺失反事实和选择偏差问题。
ai.motivation: 现有因果效应估计方法多限于点估计，缺乏分布建模能力，且难以处理选择偏差。
ai.method: 通过最小化正则化Wasserstein距离对齐处理组和对照组的表示，并引入L2归一化约束增强数值稳定性，再使用条件流匹配建模潜在结果分布。
ai.result: 在多个基准上，RepFlow在点估计和分布估计上均优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从观测数据中更准确地估计因果效应，包括点估计和完整的分布信息。

idea_spark.idea: 将表示学习与条件流匹配联合优化，以平衡表示并建模潜在结果分布。
idea_spark.risk: 流匹配模型在高维或小样本场景下可能不稳定，L2归一化可能限制表示容量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Wasserstein距离在域适应中成功应用的启发，将其用于因果推断中的选择偏差校正。

idea_spark.risk: 流匹配模型在高维或小样本场景下可能不稳定，L2归一化可能限制表示容量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Wasserstein距离在域适应中成功应用的启发，将其用于因果推断中的选择偏差校正。

en.tldr: Proposes RepFlow, a framework integrating representation learning with conditional flow matching for causal effect estimation, addressing missing counterfactuals and selection bias.
en.motivation: Existing methods are limited to point estimates and lack distribution modeling capacity, and struggle with selection bias.
en.method: Aligns treated and control representations by minimizing entropically regularized Wasserstein distance, applies L2 normalization for stability, then uses conditional flow matching to model potential outcome distributions.
en.result: Consistently outperforms existing methods on both point and distributional causal effect estimation across benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Enables more accurate causal effect estimation from observational data, including both point estimates and full distribution information.

idea_spark.idea: Jointly optimize representation learning and conditional flow matching to balance representations and model potential outcome distributions.
idea_spark.risk: Flow matching may be unstable in high-dimensional or small-sample settings; L2 normalization may limit representation capacity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of Wasserstein distance in domain adaptation, applied here to correct selection bias in causal inference.

papers.title: FREPix: Frequency-Heterogeneous Flow Matching for Pixel-Space Image Generation
papers.summary: Pixel-space diffusion has re-emerged as a promising alternative to latent-space generation because it avoids the representation bottleneck introduced by VAEs. Yet most existing methods still treat image generation as a frequency-homogeneous process, overlooking the distinct roles and learning dynamics of low- and high-frequency components. To address this, we propose FREPix, a FREquency-heterogeneous flow matching framework for Pixel-space image generation. FREPix explicitly decomposes generation into low- and high-frequency components, assigns them separate transport paths, predicts them with a factorized network, and trains them with a frequency-aware objective. In this way, coarse-to-fine generation becomes an explicit design principle rather than an implicit behavior. On ImageNet class-to-image generation, FREPix achieves competitive results among pixel-space generation models, reaching 1.91 FID at $256\times256$ and 2.38 FID at $512\times512$, with particularly strong behavior in the low-NFE regime.

ai.tldr: 提出FREPix，一种频率异质流匹配框架，在像素空间中将图像生成显式分解为低频和高频分量，实现从粗到细的生成。
ai.motivation: 现有像素空间扩散方法将图像生成视为频率同质过程，忽略了低高频分量的不同角色和学习动态。
ai.method: 将生成分解为低高频分量，分配独立传输路径，使用分解网络预测，并采用频率感知目标训练。
ai.result: 在ImageNet上，256×256分辨率下FID为1.91，512×512下FID为2.38，在低NFE场景下表现突出。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种显式利用频率信息进行像素空间图像生成的方法，可提升生成质量和效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将频率分解与流匹配结合，设计因子化网络分别处理不同频率分量，实现显式粗到细生成。
idea_spark.risk: 分解可能引入额外计算开销，且低高频分量的最优划分需进一步探索。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受图像处理中频率分解启发，将生成过程显式建模为多尺度过程，而非隐式学习。

en.tldr: Proposes FREPix, a frequency-heterogeneous flow matching framework that explicitly decomposes pixel-space image generation into low- and high-frequency components for coarse-to-fine generation.
en.motivation: Existing pixel-space diffusion methods treat image generation as a frequency-homogeneous process, overlooking distinct roles and learning dynamics of low- and high-frequency components.

en.help_to_user: Provides a method that explicitly leverages frequency information for pixel-space image generation, improving quality and efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Combines frequency decomposition with flow matching, designing a factorized network to handle different frequency components for explicit coarse-to-fine generation.
idea_spark.risk: Decomposition may introduce extra computational cost, and optimal partitioning of low/high frequencies needs further exploration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by frequency decomposition in image processing, explicitly models generation as a multi-scale process rather than implicit learning.

papers.title: SymDrift: One-Shot Generative Modeling under Symmetries
papers.summary: Generative modeling of physical systems, such as molecules, requires learning distributions that are invariant under global symmetries, such as rotations in three-dimensional space. Equivariant diffusion and flow matching models can incorporate such invariances effectively, even when trained on a non-invariant empirical distribution, but they typically rely on costly multi-step sampling. Recently, drifting models have emerged as an efficient alternative, enabling single-step generation and achieving state-of-the-art performance in generative modeling tasks. However, we show that drifting models face a symmetry-specific challenge, since an equivariant generator does not generally produce the same drifting field as the one obtained from the symmetrized target distribution. Addressing this issue would require expensive symmetrization of the empirical distribution. To avoid this cost, we propose SymDrift, a framework that makes the drifting field itself symmetry-aware. We introduce two complementary strategies: (i) a symmetrized drift in coordinate space based on optimal alignment, and (ii) a $G$-invariant embedding that removes symmetry ambiguity by construction. Empirically, SymDrift outperforms existing one-shot methods on standard benchmarks for conformer and transition state generation, while remaining competitive with significantly more expensive multi-step approaches. By enabling one-shot inference, SymDrift reduces computational overhead by up to 40$\times$ compared to existing baselines, making it promising for high-throughput applications such as virtual drug screening and large-scale reaction network exploration.

ai.tldr: 提出SymDrift框架，通过对称性感知的漂移场实现单步生成，避免昂贵的对称化处理，在分子构象和过渡态生成任务上超越现有单步方法，计算开销降低40倍。
ai.motivation: 现有等变扩散/流匹配模型需多步采样，而漂移模型虽能单步生成，但面临对称性挑战：等变生成器产生的漂移场与对称化目标分布不一致，需昂贵对称化。
ai.method: 提出两种互补策略：(i) 基于最优对齐的坐标空间对称化漂移；(ii) G-不变嵌入，从构造上消除对称歧义。
ai.result: 在标准分子构象和过渡态生成基准上，SymDrift优于现有单步方法，与多步方法竞争力相当，推理速度提升40倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不牺牲对称性的前提下实现高效单步生成，适用于高通量虚拟筛选和反应网络探索。

idea_spark.idea: 利用最优对齐和不变嵌入，将对称性先验直接融入单步生成器的训练目标。
idea_spark.risk: 最优对齐可能引入额外计算开销，且G-不变嵌入的设计对特定对称群依赖性强。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从等变网络与漂移模型的矛盾出发，通过对称性感知的损失函数实现高效生成。

en.tldr: SymDrift enables one-shot generative modeling under symmetries by making the drifting field symmetry-aware, avoiding costly symmetrization, and achieving up to 40x speedup on molecular conformer and transition state generation.
en.motivation: Equivariant diffusion/flow matching models require multi-step sampling; drifting models offer single-step generation but suffer from a symmetry mismatch between the equivariant generator and the symmetrized target distribution, necessitating expensive symmetrization.
en.method: Two complementary strategies: (i) symmetrized drift in coordinate space via optimal alignment, and (ii) a G-invariant embedding that removes symmetry ambiguity by construction.
en.result: Outperforms existing one-shot methods on standard conformer and transition state benchmarks, competitive with multi-step approaches, and reduces computational overhead by up to 40x.
en.help_to_user: Helps users achieve efficient single-step generation without sacrificing symmetry, suitable for high-throughput virtual screening and reaction network exploration.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate symmetry priors directly into the training objective of a one-shot generator via optimal alignment and invariant embeddings.
idea_spark.risk: Optimal alignment may introduce extra computation; G-invariant embedding design is group-specific.
idea_spark.inspiration: Addressing the symmetry challenge in drifting models by making the drift itself symmetry-aware, inspired by the conflict between equivariance and single-step generation.

papers.title: Relaxed Sparsest-Permutation Formulation for Causal Discovery at Scale
papers.summary: Despite the growing availability of large datasets, causal structure learning remains computationally prohibitive at scale. We revisit sparsest-permutation learning for linear structural equation models and show that exact Cholesky factorization is unnecessary for structure recovery. This observation motivates a support-level relaxation that searches for sparse triangular factors over a precision-support screening graph. The relaxed formulation can be efficiently evaluated via masked zero-fill incomplete Cholesky factorization, enabling scalable comparison of candidate orderings. At the population level, we establish soundness for Markov equivalence class (MEC) recovery under no-cancellation and sparsest Markov representation assumptions, as well as robustness to ordering misspecification. Motivated by these guarantees, we introduce SCOPE, a sparse-Cholesky pipeline that provides a scalable implementation of the relaxed formulation. Experiments on synthetic and real datasets demonstrate that SCOPE matches the MEC recovery accuracy of substantially slower baselines, while achieving significantly reduced runtime and scaling to 10k variables.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于稀疏置换的因果结构学习松弛公式，通过掩码零填充不完全Cholesky分解实现大规模变量（10k）的高效因果发现。
ai.motivation: 现有因果结构学习方法在大规模数据集上计算成本过高，难以实用。
ai.method: 重新审视线性结构方程模型中的最稀疏置换学习，利用支持级松弛在精度支持筛选图上搜索稀疏三角因子，并通过掩码零填充不完全Cholesky分解高效评估候选排序。
ai.result: 在合成和真实数据集上，SCOPE方法在保持马尔可夫等价类恢复精度的同时，显著降低运行时间，可扩展至10k变量。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种可扩展的因果发现工具，适用于高维数据分析场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用不完全Cholesky分解近似替代精确分解，在精度与效率间取得平衡。
idea_spark.risk: 在强因果效应或密集图场景下，松弛可能导致结构恢复偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数值线性代数中借用不完全分解技术，启发因果发现算法的加速设计。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从数值线性代数中借用不完全分解技术，启发因果发现算法的加速设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a relaxed sparsest-permutation formulation for causal discovery, enabling scalable structure learning up to 10k variables via masked zero-fill incomplete Cholesky factorization.
en.motivation: Existing causal structure learning methods are computationally prohibitive for large-scale datasets.
en.method: Revisits sparsest-permutation learning for linear SEMs, introduces a support-level relaxation that searches for sparse triangular factors over a precision-support screening graph, and evaluates candidate orderings efficiently via masked zero-fill incomplete Cholesky factorization.
en.result: On synthetic and real datasets, SCOPE matches MEC recovery accuracy of slower baselines while achieving significantly reduced runtime and scaling to 10k variables.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable causal discovery tool suitable for high-dimensional data analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Approximate exact factorization with incomplete Cholesky to balance accuracy and efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Under strong causal effects or dense graphs, the relaxation may introduce bias in structure recovery.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrowing incomplete factorization techniques from numerical linear algebra inspires acceleration of causal discovery algorithms.

papers.title: Graph Normalization: Fast Binarizing Dynamics for Differentiable MWIS
papers.summary: We introduce Graph Normalization (GN), a principled dynamical system on graphs that serves as a differentiable approximation engine for the NP-hard Maximum Weight Independent Set (MWIS) problem. MWIS encompasses many combinatorial challenges, including optimal assignment, scheduling, set packing, and MAP inference in discrete Markov Random Fields. Unlike Belief Propagation, we prove GN always converges to a binary indicator of a Maximum Independent Set. GN realizes a fast quasi-Newton descent through an exact Majorization-Minimization step, systematically improving the MWIS relaxed primal objective. We establish an equivalence between GN and the Replicator Dynamics of a nonlinear evolutionary game, where vertices compete for inclusion in an independent set. While a non-potential game, the GN game follows Fisher's Fundamental Theorem of Natural Selection, where the average fitness equals the MWIS primal objective and strictly increases. This connection leads to a weighted extension of the Motzkin-Straus theorem, showing MISes are in bijection with the local minima of a quadratic form over a tilted simplex. For the Assignment Problem, GN acts as a variant of the Sinkhorn algorithm that naturally converges to a hard assignment while generalizing to arbitrary constraint graphs. We demonstrate GN's performance as a fast binarization engine for the state-of-the-art Bregman-Sinkhorn relaxed MWIS solver. On real-world benchmarks with up to 1M edges, GN identifies solutions within 1% of the best known results in seconds on a CPU. GN opens new avenues for deep learning architectures requiring differentiable, "hard" decisions under constraints, with applications in structured sparse attention, dynamic network pruning, and Mixture-of-Experts. Beyond core AI, the GN framework enables end-to-end learning of constrained optimization in computer vision, computational biology, and resource allocation.

ai.tldr: 提出图归一化（GN）方法，一种可微分的动力系统，用于逼近NP难的MWIS问题，并证明其收敛到最大独立集的二值指示。
ai.motivation: MWIS问题在组合优化中广泛存在，但现有方法如置信传播无法保证收敛，且缺乏可微性，难以集成到深度学习框架中。
ai.method: 通过精确的Majorization-Minimization步骤实现快速拟牛顿下降，并建立与非线性进化博弈中复制动力学的等价性，利用Fisher基本定理保证平均适应度严格递增。
ai.result: 在多达100万边的真实基准上，GN在CPU上数秒内找到与已知最优解相差1%以内的解，并作为Bregman-Sinkhorn松弛求解器的快速二值化引擎。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将NP难组合优化问题转化为可微分的深度学习模块，实现端到端学习。

idea_spark.idea: 将图归一化与进化博弈理论结合，为组合优化提供新的理论视角和算法设计思路。
idea_spark.risk: 在大规模图上，动力系统的收敛速度可能受限于图结构，且对初始条件敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Fisher基本定理和Motzkin-Straus定理启发，将优化过程视为自然选择，为设计可微优化器提供新范式。

idea_spark.idea: 将图归一化与进化博弈理论结合，为组合优化提供新的理论视角和算法设计思路。
idea_spark.risk: 在大规模图上，动力系统的收敛速度可能受限于图结构，且对初始条件敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Fisher基本定理和Motzkin-Straus定理启发，将优化过程视为自然选择，为设计可微优化器提供新范式。

en.tldr: Introduces Graph Normalization (GN), a differentiable dynamical system for approximating the NP-hard MWIS problem, proven to converge to a binary indicator of a Maximum Independent Set.
en.motivation: MWIS is pervasive in combinatorial optimization, but existing methods like Belief Propagation lack convergence guarantees and differentiability, hindering integration into deep learning.
en.method: Achieves fast quasi-Newton descent via exact Majorization-Minimization, and establishes equivalence with Replicator Dynamics in evolutionary game theory, where Fisher's Fundamental Theorem ensures strict increase of average fitness.
en.result: On real-world benchmarks with up to 1M edges, GN finds solutions within 1% of best known results in seconds on a CPU, and serves as a fast binarization engine for the Bregman-Sinkhorn relaxed MWIS solver.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to transform NP-hard combinatorial optimization into differentiable deep learning modules for end-to-end learning.

idea_spark.idea: Combining graph normalization with evolutionary game theory offers a new theoretical perspective and algorithmic design for combinatorial optimization.
idea_spark.risk: Convergence speed may depend on graph structure and initial conditions, especially on large-scale graphs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Fisher's Fundamental Theorem and the Motzkin-Straus theorem, viewing optimization as natural selection provides a new paradigm for designing differentiable optimizers.

papers.title: Exponential mixing for the stochastic Allen--Cahn equation with localized white noise
papers.summary: This paper studies the 1D stochastic Allen--Cahn equation on a bounded domain driven by localized white noise. We prove that the associated Markov process admits a unique invariant measure and is exponential mixing. The main challenge lies in the interaction between localized nature of the noise and non-trivial global dynamics of the system. To overcome this, our approach relies on two ingredients from PDE control theory: stabilization for the linearized system and global steady-state controllability for the nonlinear equation. The stabilization result is derived using the weak observability and Fenchel--Rockafellar duality, while the global controllability relies on quasi-static deformations combined with global dynamics.

ai.tldr: 研究一维有界域上由局部白噪声驱动的随机Allen-Cahn方程，证明其马尔可夫过程存在唯一不变测度且指数混合。
ai.motivation: 局部噪声与系统全局非平凡动力学的相互作用是主要挑战，现有理论难以直接处理。
ai.method: 结合PDE控制理论的两个工具：线性化系统的镇定（利用弱可观测性和Fenchel-Rockafellar对偶）和非线性方程的全局稳态可控性（基于准静态变形与全局动力学）。
ai.result: 证明了唯一不变测度的存在性和指数混合性质。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解局部噪声驱动的随机偏微分方程的长期行为提供了严格的分析框架。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将准静态变形技术应用于更复杂的非线性系统（如Cahn-Hilliard方程）的全局可控性分析。
idea_spark.risk: 高维情形下弱可观测性条件可能难以验证，且准静态变形对边界条件敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从PDE控制理论借力解决随机动力学的混合问题，启发交叉领域新思路。

zh.motivation: 局部噪声与系统全局非平凡动力学的相互作用是主要挑战，现有理论难以直接处理。

zh.help_to_user: 为理解局部噪声驱动的随机偏微分方程的长期行为提供了严格的分析框架。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将准静态变形技术应用于更复杂的非线性系统（如Cahn-Hilliard方程）的全局可控性分析。
idea_spark.risk: 高维情形下弱可观测性条件可能难以验证，且准静态变形对边界条件敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从PDE控制理论借力解决随机动力学的混合问题，启发交叉领域新思路。

en.tldr: Studies the 1D stochastic Allen-Cahn equation driven by localized white noise on a bounded domain, proving existence of a unique invariant measure and exponential mixing.
en.motivation: The main challenge is the interaction between localized noise and nontrivial global dynamics, which existing theory cannot handle directly.
en.method: Combines two PDE control theory tools: stabilization of the linearized system (via weak observability and Fenchel-Rockafellar duality) and global steady-state controllability for the nonlinear equation (via quasi-static deformations and global dynamics).
en.result: Proves existence of a unique invariant measure and exponential mixing.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous analytical framework for understanding long-term behavior of SPDEs driven by localized noise.

idea_spark.idea: Try applying quasi-static deformation techniques to global controllability of more complex nonlinear systems, e.g., the Cahn-Hilliard equation.
idea_spark.risk: Weak observability conditions may be hard to verify in higher dimensions, and quasi-static deformations are sensitive to boundary conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrowing tools from PDE control theory to solve mixing problems in stochastic dynamics inspires new cross-disciplinary ideas.

papers.title: ORTHOBO: Orthogonal Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization
papers.summary: Bayesian optimization is widely used for hyperparameter optimization when model evaluations are expensive; however, noisy acquisition estimates can lead to unstable decisions. We identify acquisition estimation noise as a failure mode that was previously overlooked: even when the surrogate model and acquisition target are correctly specified, finite-sample Monte Carlo error can perturb acquisition values. This can, in turn, flip candidate rankings and lead to suboptimal BO decisions. As a remedy, we aim at variance reduction and propose an orthogonal acquisition estimator that subtracts an optimally weighted score-function control variate, which yields an acquisition residual orthogonal to posterior score directions and which thus reduces Monte Carlo variance. We further introduce OrthoBO: a Bayesian optimization framework that combines our orthogonal acquisition estimator with ensemble surrogates and an outer log transformation. We show theoretically that our estimator preserves the target, leads to variance reduction, and improves pairwise ranking stability. We further verify the theoretical properties of OrthoBO through numerical experiments where our framework reduces acquisition estimation variance, stabilizes candidate rankings, and achieves strong performance. We also demonstrate the downstream utility of OrthoBO in hyperparameter optimization for neural network training and fine-tuning.

ai.tldr: 提出OrthoBO框架，通过正交采集估计器降低贝叶斯优化中的蒙特卡洛方差，稳定候选排名，提升超参数优化性能。
ai.motivation: 贝叶斯优化中采集函数的蒙特卡洛估计噪声会导致候选排名翻转和次优决策，现有方法未充分解决此问题。
ai.method: 设计正交采集估计器，减去最优加权的得分函数控制变量，使采集残差与后验得分方向正交；结合集成代理模型和外层对数变换。
ai.result: 理论证明估计器保持目标、降低方差、改善排名稳定性；数值实验验证方差降低、排名稳定，并在神经网络超参数优化中表现优异。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户更稳定、高效地进行超参数优化，尤其适用于评估代价高的模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将正交化思想用于强化学习中的价值估计或主动学习中的查询策略，以减少估计方差。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用后验得分方向构造控制变量，启发其他贝叶斯方法中的方差缩减设计。

en.tldr: Proposes OrthoBO, a Bayesian optimization framework that reduces Monte Carlo variance via an orthogonal acquisition estimator, stabilizing candidate rankings and improving hyperparameter optimization.
en.motivation: Monte Carlo noise in acquisition estimates of Bayesian optimization can flip candidate rankings and lead to suboptimal decisions, a failure mode overlooked before.
en.method: Designs an orthogonal acquisition estimator that subtracts an optimally weighted score-function control variate, making the acquisition residual orthogonal to posterior score directions; combined with ensemble surrogates and an outer log transformation.
en.result: Theoretically preserves target, reduces variance, and improves pairwise ranking stability; numerical experiments confirm variance reduction and ranking stability, with strong performance in neural network hyperparameter optimization.
en.help_to_user: Helps users perform more stable and efficient hyperparameter optimization, especially for expensive model evaluations.

idea_spark.idea: Apply orthogonalization to value estimation in reinforcement learning or query strategies in active learning to reduce estimation variance.
idea_spark.risk: Orthogonalization may introduce extra computational cost and is sensitive to the choice of control variate.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using posterior score directions to construct control variates inspires variance reduction designs in other Bayesian methods.

papers.title: Evolutionary fine tuning of quantized convolution-based deep learning models
papers.summary: Deep learning models are the most efficient models in many machine learning tasks. The main disadvantage when using them in IoT, mobile devices, independent autonomous or real-time systems is their complexity and memory size. Therefore, much research has concentrated on compression techniques of deep learning architectures. One of the most popular technique is quantization. In most of the works, the quantization is done based on the nearest neighbour quantization technique. This work focuses on improving the quantization efficiency in pretrained and quantized models. This approach has the potential to improve the final accuracy of quantized models. The main postulate of the work is that final quantization states of the network based on nearest neighbour rounding does not guarantee optimal accuracy. In the presented work, the evolution strategy is used as an optimization approach. The evolution in each iteration changes the values of the small percentage of weights. It shifts theirs values to different quantization states. The work shows that proposed evolution with an appropriate set of operators and parameters can fast improve the accuracy of the quantized models. The results are presented for popular architectures such as VGG and Resnet for image classification and detection. Additionally, simulations were carried out for the autoencoder architecture.

ai.tldr: 提出一种进化策略优化方法，对预训练量化模型进行微调，通过迭代改变少量权重的量化状态，提升模型精度。
ai.motivation: 最近邻量化方法无法保证量化模型的最优精度，需要更有效的优化手段。
ai.method: 采用进化策略，每轮迭代改变少量权重的量化状态，配合合适的操作符和参数，快速提升量化模型精度。
ai.result: 在VGG、ResNet等图像分类检测架构以及自编码器上验证，所提方法能快速提高量化模型精度。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用进化策略优化量化深度学习模型，提升边缘设备上的模型性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将进化策略与混合精度量化结合，自动搜索最优量化位宽分配。
idea_spark.risk: 进化策略计算开销可能较大，需平衡优化速度与精度提升。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物进化启发，将权重状态视为基因，通过变异和选择寻找最优量化配置。

en.tldr: Proposes an evolutionary strategy to fine-tune pretrained quantized models by iteratively shifting a small percentage of weights to different quantization states, improving accuracy. Validated on VGG, ResNet, and autoencoder architectures.
en.motivation: Nearest neighbor quantization does not guarantee optimal accuracy for quantized models, necessitating more effective optimization.
en.method: Uses an evolution strategy that, each iteration, changes a small percentage of weights to different quantization states with appropriate operators and parameters.
en.result: Experiments on VGG, ResNet for image classification/detection and autoencoders show rapid accuracy improvement of quantized models.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage evolutionary strategies to optimize quantized deep learning models, enhancing performance on edge devices.

idea_spark.idea: Combine evolution strategy with mixed-precision quantization to automatically search for optimal bit-width allocation.
idea_spark.risk: Evolutionary optimization may incur high computational cost, requiring a trade-off between speed and accuracy gain.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological evolution, treating weight states as genes and using mutation and selection to find optimal quantization configurations.

papers.title: Molecules Meet Language: Confound-Aware Representation Learning and Chemical Property Steering in Transformer-VAE Latent Spaces
papers.summary: Molecular generative models often assume meaningful latent geometry, but apparent property predictability can reflect sequence-level shortcuts rather than chemical organization. We study this issue in an unsupervised autoregressive Transformer-VAE trained on SELFIES. After training, we freeze the model, fit linear probes to RDKit descriptors, and use the probe weights as candidate global steering directions. To separate chemical signal from SELFIES artifacts, we introduce a confound-aware evaluation based on residualization, confound-direction alignment analysis, and decoded-molecule traversal. This is necessary because SELFIES length, branch tokens, ring tokens, and token entropy are strongly encoded in the latent space. Under this confound-aware evaluation, we find robust monotonic steering for cLogP, FractionCSP3, HeavyAtomCount, TPSA, BertzCT, and HBA. Nonlinear probes further show that some properties admit stable global directions, while others are better described by local latent gradients. Overall, our results show that chemically meaningful steering can emerge in entangled molecular latent spaces, but only when validated through decoded molecules and controlled for representation-level confounds.

ai.tldr: 在无监督Transformer-VAE的SELFIES潜空间中，通过残差化等混淆感知评估发现，化学性质的可控编辑是可能的，但必须排除序列伪影的干扰。
ai.motivation: 分子生成模型常假设潜空间具有有意义的几何结构，但性质的可预测性可能源于序列层面的捷径而非真正的化学组织。
ai.method: 在SELFIES上训练无监督自回归Transformer-VAE，冻结模型后用线性探针拟合RDKit描述符，并通过残差化、混淆方向对齐分析和解码分子遍历进行混淆感知评估。
ai.result: cLogP、FractionCSP3、HeavyAtomCount、TPSA、BertzCT和HBA表现出稳健的单调编辑能力；非线性探针显示部分性质存在全局方向，另一些则更适合局部梯度描述。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在分子潜空间中区分真正的化学信号与序列伪影，并指导可靠的化学性质编辑。

idea_spark.idea: 利用残差化后的潜空间方向进行分子优化，同时设计对抗性训练以显式消除序列混淆。
idea_spark.risk: 混淆方向可能随模型架构或训练数据变化，需重新校准；解码分子遍历的计算成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将混淆控制与可解释性结合，开发能自动识别并抑制序列伪影的分子生成模型。

en.tldr: In an unsupervised Transformer-VAE latent space on SELFIES, confound-aware evaluation via residualization reveals that chemical property steering is feasible but must be validated against sequence-level artifacts.
en.motivation: Molecular generative models often assume meaningful latent geometry, but property predictability may stem from sequence shortcuts rather than chemical organization.
en.method: Train an unsupervised autoregressive Transformer-VAE on SELFIES, freeze the model, fit linear probes to RDKit descriptors, and evaluate with residualization, confound-direction alignment, and decoded-molecule traversal.
en.result: Robust monotonic steering for cLogP, FractionCSP3, HeavyAtomCount, TPSA, BertzCT, and HBA; nonlinear probes show some properties have global directions while others require local gradients.
en.help_to_user: Helps users distinguish true chemical signals from sequence artifacts in molecular latent spaces and guides reliable property steering.

idea_spark.idea: Use residualized latent directions for molecular optimization and design adversarial training to explicitly remove sequence confounds.
idea_spark.risk: Confound directions may vary with model architecture or training data, requiring recalibration; decoded-molecule traversal is computationally expensive.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine confound control with interpretability to develop molecular generative models that automatically detect and suppress sequence artifacts.

papers.title: A Flow Matching Algorithm for Many-Shot Adaptation to Unseen Distributions
papers.summary: While generative modeling has achieved remarkable success on tasks like natural language-conditioned image generation, enabling model adaptation from example data points remains a relatively underexplored and challenging problem. To this end, we propose Function Projection for Flow Matching (FP-FM), an algorithm that directly conditions generation on samples from the target distribution. FP-FM learns basis functions to span the velocity fields corresponding to a set of training distributions, and adapts to new distributions by computing a simple least-squares projection onto this basis. This enables efficient generation of samples from diverse target distributions without additional training at inference time. We further introduce multiple variants of FP-FM that provide a trade-off in expressivity and compute by enriching the coefficient calculation, e.g., by making the coefficients dependent on time. FP-FM achieves greatly improved precision and recall relative to baselines across synthetic and image-based datasets, with especially strong gains on unseen distributions.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于流匹配的函数投影算法，通过最小二乘投影实现对新分布的高效适应，无需额外训练。
ai.motivation: 现有生成模型在从示例数据点进行模型适应方面探索不足，需要一种无需额外训练即可适应新分布的方法。
ai.method: 学习一组基函数来张成训练分布的速度场，对新分布通过最小二乘投影计算系数，并引入时间依赖等变体以平衡表达力与计算量。
ai.result: 在合成和图像数据集上，FP-FM在精度和召回率上显著优于基线，尤其对未见分布表现突出。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需重新训练即可从少量样本生成新分布样本的实用方法。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受函数逼近和迁移学习中基函数投影思想的启发。

en.tldr: Proposes a flow matching algorithm that adapts to unseen distributions via least-squares projection onto learned basis functions, requiring no additional training.
en.motivation: Generative models lack efficient adaptation from example data points; need a method that adapts to new distributions without retraining.
en.method: Learns basis functions spanning velocity fields of training distributions; adapts to new distributions via least-squares projection, with time-dependent coefficient variants for expressivity-compute trade-off.
en.result: FP-FM achieves significantly improved precision and recall on synthetic and image datasets, especially on unseen distributions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical way to generate samples from new distributions using only a few examples, without retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Project velocity fields onto pre-trained basis functions for zero-shot distribution adaptation.
idea_spark.risk: Basis functions may not fully capture complex velocity fields, limiting adaptation quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by function approximation and basis projection in transfer learning.

papers.title: Asymptotic properties of solutions to the characteristic problem for the ultrahyperbolic equation
papers.summary: The paper concerns the problem for the ultrahyperbolic equation in the Euclidean space with data on a characteristic hyperplane. Smoothness and asymptotics of the solution along characteristic lines transversal to the initial hyperplane are investigated.

ai.tldr: 研究了超双曲型方程在特征超平面上的特征问题，分析了沿特征线的光滑性和渐近行为。
ai.motivation: 理解超双曲型方程在特征超平面上解的性质，特别是沿特征线的渐近行为。
ai.method: 在欧几里得空间中，将数据置于特征超平面上，研究解沿横向特征线的光滑性和渐近展开。
ai.result: 获得了解沿特征线的光滑性条件和渐近展开形式。
ai.help_to_user: 为超双曲型方程的特征问题提供了理论分析工具，可用于相关偏微分方程研究。

idea_spark.idea: 将特征超平面上的数据与沿特征线的渐近行为结合，可能发展出新的数值解法。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果可能依赖于特定的几何结构，推广需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将特征线方法用于高维波动方程或量子场论中的传播子分析。
