en.tldr: An end-to-end neuromorphic architecture integrating a spiking visual attention mechanism and a compact spiking neural network on SpiNNaker for low-latency, energy-efficient American Sign Language fingerspelling recognition.
en.motivation: Existing sign-language recognition methods suffer from high latency and power consumption, limiting real-time deployment; neuromorphic sensing and processing offer a sparse, event-driven alternative for low-latency and energy-efficient perception.
en.method: Integrates a spiking visual attention mechanism for online region-of-interest extraction with a compact spiking neural network deployed on the SpiNNaker neuromorphic platform.
en.result: Achieves 92.27% accuracy in simulation, 83.1% on hardware, with power consumption of 0.565 mW and latency of 3 ms, outperforming all benchmarked approaches in energy efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates the feasibility and efficiency of neuromorphic visual attention for edge-based sign-language recognition, providing a reference for low-power real-time gesture interaction.

idea_spark.idea: Combine spiking visual attention with more complex spiking neural networks (e.g., spiking convolutional LSTMs) to improve dynamic gesture sequence recognition.
idea_spark.risk: The accuracy drop from simulation to hardware deployment may stem from neuromorphic hardware precision limits or noise; further network optimization or hardware calibration is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological visual attention, future work could explore multimodal spiking fusion (e.g., vision + electromyography) to enhance robustness.

papers.title: Jointly Learning Structured Representations and Stabilized Affinity for Human Motion Segmentation
papers.summary: Human Motion Segmentation (HMS), which aims to partition a video into non-overlapping segments corresponding to different human motions, has recently attracted increasing research attention. Existing HMS approaches are predominantly based on subspace clustering, which are grounded on the assumption that the distribution of high-dimensional temporal features well aligns with a Union-of-Subspaces (UoS). For videos in the real world, however, the raw frame-level features often violate the UoS assumption and yield unsatisfactory segmentation performance. To address this issue, we propose an efficient and effective approach for HMS, named Temporal Deep Self-expressive subspace Clustering (TDSC), which jointly learns temporally consistent structured representations and stabilized affinity for accurate and robust HMS. Specifically, in TDSC, we alternately learn structured representations of the input frame features and self-expressive coefficients via a properly regularized self-expressive model, in which a coding-rate maximization regularizer is incorporated to avoid representation collapse and conform the learned representations to span a desired UoS distribution, and meanwhile, temporal constraints are incorporated to promote temporally adjacent frames to be partitioned into the same groups. Moreover, we develop a temporal momentum averaging mechanism to stabilize affinity evolution and design a reparameterization strategy to enable efficient optimization. We conduct extensive experiments on five benchmark HMS datasets using both conventional (HoG) and up-to-date deep features (i.e., CLIP, DINOv2) to validate the effectiveness of our approach.

ai.tldr: 提出一种联合学习结构化表示与稳定亲和度的人体运动分割方法，通过自表达模型、时序约束和动量平均机制提升分割精度与鲁棒性。
ai.motivation: 现有基于子空间聚类的运动分割方法假设帧特征符合子空间并集，但真实视频中帧级特征常违背该假设，导致分割性能不佳。
ai.method: 交替学习输入帧特征的结构化表示和自表达系数，引入编码率最大化正则化避免表示坍缩，加入时序约束促进相邻帧分组一致，并采用时序动量平均机制稳定亲和度演化。
ai.result: 在五个基准数据集上使用传统HoG特征和现代深度特征（CLIP、DINOv2）进行实验，验证了方法的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种鲁棒且高效的人体运动分割方案，适用于视频分析、动作识别等场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将自表达模型与对比学习结合，进一步强化帧间判别性。
idea_spark.risk: 动量平均可能引入延迟，对快速运动变化不敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用视频中自然存在的时序平滑性作为弱监督信号，减少对密集标注的依赖。

en.tldr: Proposes a method that jointly learns structured representations and stabilized affinity for human motion segmentation, using a self-expressive model with temporal constraints and momentum averaging to improve accuracy and robustness.
en.motivation: Existing subspace clustering methods assume frame features lie in a union of subspaces, but real-world videos violate this assumption, leading to poor segmentation.
en.method: Alternately learns structured representations and self-expressive coefficients; incorporates a coding-rate maximization regularizer to avoid representation collapse, temporal constraints to enforce grouping consistency, and a temporal momentum averaging mechanism to stabilize affinity evolution.
en.result: Extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets with both traditional (HoG) and modern deep features (CLIP, DINOv2) validate the effectiveness.
en.help_to_user: Provides a robust and efficient solution for human motion segmentation, useful for video analysis and action recognition.

idea_spark.idea: Combine self-expressive model with contrastive learning to further enhance inter-frame discriminability.
idea_spark.risk: Momentum averaging may introduce latency and be insensitive to rapid motion changes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage natural temporal smoothness in videos as a weak supervision signal to reduce reliance on dense annotations.

papers.title: Metonymy in vision models undermines attention-based interpretability
papers.summary: Part-based reasoning is a classical strategy to make a computer vision model directly focus on the object parts that are relevant to the downstream task. In the context of deep learning, this also serves to improve by-design interpretability, often by using part-centric attention mechanisms on top of a latent image representation provided by a standard, black-box model. This approach is based on a locality assumption: that the latent representation of an object part encodes primarily information about the corresponding image region. In this work, we test this basic assumption, measuring intra-object leakage in vision models using part-based attribute annotations. Through a comprehensive experimental evaluation, we show that modern pretrained vision transformers violate the locality assumption and exhibit a strong intra-object leakage, in which each part encodes information from the whole object, a visual metonymy that compromises the faithfulness of attention-based interpretable-by-design methods for part-based reasoning, ultimately rendering them uninterpretable. In addition, we establish an upper bound using a two-stage approach that prevents leakage by design. We then show that this inherently disentangled feature extraction improves attribute-driven part discovery on a variety of tasks, confirming the practical impact of intra-object leakage. Our results uncover a neglected issue affecting the interpretability of part-based representations, such as those in CBMs relying on part-centric concepts, highlighting that two-stage approaches offer a promising way to mitigate it.

ai.tldr: 现代视觉Transformer存在内部对象泄漏，每个部分编码了整个对象的信息，破坏了基于注意力的可解释性。
ai.motivation: 基于部分推理的可解释性方法依赖于局部性假设，即对象部分的潜在表示主要编码对应图像区域的信息。本文旨在检验这一假设是否成立。
ai.method: 使用基于部分属性的标注，测量视觉模型中内部对象泄漏的程度，并设计两阶段方法防止泄漏。
ai.result: 预训练视觉Transformer严重违反局部性假设，导致视觉转喻，使注意力机制不可解释；两阶段方法可有效防止泄漏，提升属性驱动的部分发现性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解基于注意力的可解释性方法的局限性，并提供改进方向。

idea_spark.idea: 设计显式防止特征泄漏的架构，例如分离对象级和部分级编码器。
idea_spark.risk: 两阶段方法可能增加计算开销，且解耦程度需精细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从视觉转喻现象出发，重新思考可解释性评估中的基本假设。

idea_spark.risk: 两阶段方法可能增加计算开销，且解耦程度需精细调参。

en.tldr: Modern vision transformers exhibit intra-object leakage where each part encodes whole-object information, undermining attention-based interpretability.
en.motivation: Part-based interpretability relies on the locality assumption that a part's latent representation primarily encodes its corresponding region. This work tests that assumption.
en.method: Measured intra-object leakage in vision models using part-based attribute annotations, and proposed a two-stage approach to prevent leakage by design.
en.result: Pretrained vision transformers strongly violate the locality assumption, causing visual metonymy that renders attention mechanisms uninterpretable; the two-stage approach effectively prevents leakage and improves attribute-driven part discovery.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the limitations of attention-based interpretability methods and provides a promising mitigation direction.

idea_spark.idea: Design architectures that explicitly prevent feature leakage, e.g., separating object-level and part-level encoders.
idea_spark.risk: Two-stage methods may increase computational cost and require careful tuning of disentanglement strength.
idea_spark.inspiration: The visual metonymy phenomenon inspires rethinking fundamental assumptions in interpretability evaluation.

papers.title: Tumor-aware augmentation with task-guided attention analysis improves rectal cancer segmentation from magnetic resonance images
papers.summary: Pretraining on large-scale datasets has been shown to improve transformer generalizability, even for out-of-domain (OOD) modalities and tasks. However, two common assumptions often fail under OOD transfer: that downstream datasets can be adapted to the fixed input geometry of pretrained models and that pretrained representations transfer effectively across imaging modalities. We show that these assumptions break down through two interacting failure modes in CT-to-MRI transfer: inefficient token usage caused by zero-padding to match pretrained input dimensions and ineffective feature adaptation. These failures led to accuracy degradation despite extensive fine-tuning. We investigated these failure modes using two CT-pretrained hierarchical shifted-window transformer backbones, SMIT and Swin UNETR, pretrained with different objectives and datasets. Mechanistic analysis introduced an attention dilution index (ADI), an entropy-based metric quantifying attention diverted toward uninformative padding tokens, and centered kernel alignment (CKA) to measure feature reuse in MRI tasks. ADI increased with zero-padding, while high feature reuse did not necessarily correspond to improved accuracy. To mitigate these issues, we introduced two interventions: a tumor-aware augmentation strategy to improve tumor appearance heterogeneity coverage and an anisotropic cropping strategy to restore token efficiency. Fine-tuning on identical rectal MRI datasets improved detection rates to 224/247 (90.7%) for SMIT and 219/247 (88.7%) for Swin UNETR, demonstrating improved robustness under CT-to-MRI transfer. This study is among the first to examine when pretrained transformers fail to transfer effectively across imaging modalities and how simple mitigation strategies, motivated by mechanistic analysis of datasets, can reduce transfer limitations while improving robustness and MRI detection.

ai.tldr: 提出肿瘤感知增强和任务引导注意力分析，改善CT预训练Transformer在MRI直肠癌分割中的迁移效果。
ai.motivation: CT预训练模型迁移到MRI时存在零填充导致token效率低下和特征适应不良两个失效模式，影响分割精度。
ai.method: 引入注意力稀释指数(ADI)和中心核对齐(CKA)分析失效机制，设计肿瘤感知增强和各项异性裁剪策略。
ai.result: 在直肠MRI数据集上，SMIT检测率90.7%，Swin UNETR检测率88.7%，优于基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供跨模态迁移失效的量化诊断工具和简单有效的缓解策略。

idea_spark.idea: 利用肿瘤感知增强生成更丰富的肿瘤外观变化，提升模型对异质性的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 增强策略可能引入伪影或过度拟合特定肿瘤形态。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数据分布和注意力机制两个角度联合优化迁移学习，而非仅依赖模型微调。

en.tldr: Proposes tumor-aware augmentation and task-guided attention analysis to improve CT-pretrained transformer transfer for rectal cancer segmentation from MRI.
en.motivation: CT-to-MRI transfer suffers from zero-padding token inefficiency and ineffective feature adaptation, degrading accuracy despite fine-tuning.
en.method: Introduces attention dilution index (ADI) and centered kernel alignment (CKA) to diagnose failures, then applies tumor-aware augmentation and anisotropic cropping.
en.result: On rectal MRI dataset, SMIT achieves 90.7% detection rate and Swin UNETR 88.7%, outperforming baselines.
en.help_to_user: Provides diagnostic metrics and simple mitigation strategies for cross-modal transfer failures.

idea_spark.idea: Use tumor-aware augmentation to enrich tumor appearance heterogeneity for better robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Augmentation may introduce artifacts or overfit specific tumor patterns.
idea_spark.inspiration: Jointly optimize data distribution and attention mechanism for transfer learning, not just fine-tuning.

papers.title: RAWild: Sensor-Agnostic RAW Object Detection via Physics-Guided Curve and Grid Modeling
papers.summary: Camera sensor RAW data offers intrinsic advantages for object detection, including deeper bit depth, preserved physical information, and freedom from image signal processor (ISP) distortions. However, varying exposure conditions, spectral sensitivities, and bit depths across devices introduce substantially larger domain gaps than sRGB, making sensor-agnostic generalization a fundamental challenge. In this study, we present \textbf{RAWild}, a physics-guided global-local tone mapping framework for sensor-agnostic RAW object detection. By factoring sensor-induced variations into a global tonal correction and a spatially adaptive local color adjustment, both driven by RAW distribution priors, our framework enables a single network to train jointly across heterogeneous sensors. To further support cross-sensor generalization, we construct a physics-based RAW simulation pipeline that synthesizes realistic sensor outputs spanning diverse spectral sensitivities, illuminants, and sensor non-idealities. Extensive experiments across multiple RAW benchmarks covering bit depths from 10 to 24 demonstrate state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance under single-dataset, mixed-dataset, and challenging robustness settings.

ai.tldr: 提出RAWild框架，通过物理引导的全局-局部色调映射实现传感器无关的RAW图像目标检测，在多种传感器数据上达到最优性能。
ai.motivation: 不同相机传感器的RAW数据在曝光、光谱灵敏度和位深度上存在巨大域差异，导致现有方法难以泛化。
ai.method: 将传感器差异分解为全局色调校正和空间自适应局部颜色调整，两者均由RAW分布先验驱动；并构建基于物理的RAW模拟管道生成多样化传感器输出。
ai.result: 在多个RAW基准（10-24位深度）上，单数据集、混合数据集和鲁棒性设置下均取得最优结果。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用RAW数据的物理特性实现跨传感器目标检测，减少对ISP的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将全局-局部色调映射与自监督学习结合，进一步减少对标注数据的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟管道可能无法完全覆盖真实传感器的非线性噪声，导致实际部署时性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从RAW数据的物理先验出发，将域差异显式建模为可解释的组件，启发其他视觉任务中的域泛化设计。

zh.result: 在多个RAW基准（10-24位深度）上，单数据集、混合数据集和鲁棒性设置下均取得最优结果。

idea_spark.risk: 模拟管道可能无法完全覆盖真实传感器的非线性噪声，导致实际部署时性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从RAW数据的物理先验出发，将域差异显式建模为可解释的组件，启发其他视觉任务中的域泛化设计。

en.tldr: Proposes RAWild, a physics-guided global-local tone mapping framework for sensor-agnostic RAW object detection, achieving SOTA across diverse sensors.
en.motivation: RAW data from different sensors exhibit large domain gaps in exposure, spectral sensitivity, and bit depth, hindering generalization.
en.method: Decomposes sensor variations into global tonal correction and spatially adaptive local color adjustment driven by RAW distribution priors; builds a physics-based RAW simulation pipeline for diverse sensor outputs.
en.result: Achieves SOTA on multiple RAW benchmarks (10-24 bit depth) under single-dataset, mixed-dataset, and robustness settings.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage RAW physical properties for cross-sensor object detection, reducing ISP dependency.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining global-local tone mapping with self-supervised learning to further reduce reliance on labeled data.
idea_spark.risk: The simulation pipeline may not fully cover real sensor nonlinearities, causing performance drops in deployment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explicitly modeling domain differences as interpretable components from RAW physical priors inspires domain generalization designs in other vision tasks.

papers.title: Adaptive Physical-Facial Representation Fusion via Subject-Invariant Cross-Modal Prompt Tuning for Video-Based Emotion Recognition
papers.summary: Emotion recognition from facial videos enables non-contact inference of human emotional states. Although facial expressions are widely used cues, they cannot fully reflect intrinsic affective states. Remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) provides complementary physiological information, but it is highly susceptible to noise and inter-subject variability, limiting generalization to unseen individuals. Existing multimodal methods combine facial and rPPG features, yet their fusion strategies often disrupt pretrained facial representations and lack explicit mechanisms to suppress subject-specific variations. To address these issues, we propose a subject-invariant cross-modal prompt-tuning framework for video-based emotion recognition. Specifically, rPPG waveforms are transformed into noise-robust time-frequency representations (TFRs), from which modality-complementary prompts are generated to modulate facial tokens within a frozen Vision Transformer (ViT). This design enables effective cross-modal interaction while preserving the generalizable facial representations learned by the pretrained backbone. In addition, we introduce a decoupled shared-specific adapter (DSSA) into each ViT layer to explicitly separate subject-shared and subject-specific components, thereby improving cross-subject generalization. Experiments on the MAHNOB-HCI and DEAP benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms strong baselines in both recognition accuracy and generalization ability, highlighting its effectiveness for video-based emotion recognition.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于跨模态提示调优的主体不变融合框架，结合面部视频和rPPG信号进行视频情感识别，在MAHNOB-HCI和DEAP上取得最优性能。
ai.motivation: 面部表情无法完全反映内在情感状态，rPPG信号易受噪声和个体差异影响，现有多模态融合方法破坏预训练面部表示且缺乏抑制主体变化的机制。
ai.method: 将rPPG波形转换为噪声鲁棒的时频表示，生成模态互补提示调制冻结ViT中的面部令牌；引入解耦共享-特定适配器分离主体共享与特定成分。
ai.result: 在MAHNOB-HCI和DEAP基准上，所提方法在识别准确率和泛化能力上持续优于强基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种融合面部与生理信号的情感识别新思路，尤其适用于跨主体泛化场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将rPPG时频表示作为提示，冻结预训练视觉骨干，避免微调破坏通用特征。
idea_spark.risk: rPPG信号质量对提示生成影响大，低信噪比场景下性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用冻结预训练模型+轻量提示适配器实现高效跨模态融合，减少计算开销并保留预训练知识。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于跨模态提示调优的主体不变融合框架，结合面部视频和rPPG信号进行视频情感识别，在MAHNOB-HCI和DEAP上取得最优性能。
zh.motivation: 面部表情无法完全反映内在情感状态，rPPG信号易受噪声和个体差异影响，现有多模态融合方法破坏预训练面部表示且缺乏抑制主体变化的机制。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种融合面部与生理信号的情感识别新思路，尤其适用于跨主体泛化场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将rPPG时频表示作为提示，冻结预训练视觉骨干，避免微调破坏通用特征。
idea_spark.risk: rPPG信号质量对提示生成影响大，低信噪比场景下性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用冻结预训练模型+轻量提示适配器实现高效跨模态融合，减少计算开销并保留预训练知识。

en.tldr: Proposes a subject-invariant cross-modal prompt tuning framework that fuses facial video and rPPG signals for video-based emotion recognition, achieving state-of-the-art on MAHNOB-HCI and DEAP.
en.motivation: Facial expressions alone cannot fully reflect intrinsic affective states; rPPG is noisy and subject-dependent; existing multimodal methods disrupt pretrained facial representations and lack explicit subject-variation suppression.
en.method: Transforms rPPG waveforms into noise-robust time-frequency representations to generate modality-complementary prompts that modulate facial tokens in a frozen ViT; introduces a decoupled shared-specific adapter to separate subject-shared and subject-specific components.
en.result: Consistently outperforms strong baselines in recognition accuracy and generalization on MAHNOB-HCI and DEAP benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new approach for fusing facial and physiological signals in emotion recognition, especially for cross-subject generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Use rPPG time-frequency representations as prompts to modulate a frozen pretrained visual backbone, avoiding fine-tuning that disrupts general features.
idea_spark.risk: rPPG signal quality heavily influences prompt generation; performance may degrade under low signal-to-noise conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage frozen pretrained models with lightweight prompt adapters for efficient cross-modal fusion, reducing computational cost while preserving pretrained knowledge.

papers.title: AMIEOD: Adaptive Multi-Experts Image Enhancement for Object Detection in Low-Illumination Scenes
papers.summary: In multimedia application scenarios, images captured under low-illumination conditions often lead to lower accuracy in visual perception tasks compared to those taken in well-lit environments.   To tackle this challenge, we propose AMIEOD, an image enhancement-enabled object detection framework for low-illumination scenes, where the two tasks are jointly optimized in a detection performance-oriented manner.   Specifically, to fully exploit the information in poorly lit images, a Multi-Experts Image Enhancement Module (MEIEM) is proposed, which leverages diverse enhancement strategies.   On this basis, aiming to better align the MEIEM with the detection task, we propose a Detection-Guided Regression Loss (DGRL) that utilizes the detection result to decide the regression target.   Moreover, to dynamically select the most suitable enhancement strategy from MEIEM during inference, we construct an Expert Selection Module (ESM) guided by the proposed Detection-Guided Cross-Entropy (DGCE) loss, which formulates the optimization of ESM as a classification task.   The improved method is well-matched with current detection algorithms to improve their performance in dim scenes.   Extensive experiments on multiple datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves object detection accuracy in low-illumination conditions. Our code has been released at https://github.com/scujayfantasy/AMIEOD

ai.tldr: 提出AMIEOD框架，通过多专家图像增强模块和检测导向损失函数联合优化，提升低光照场景下的目标检测精度。
ai.motivation: 低光照图像导致视觉感知任务精度下降，现有方法未充分联合优化增强与检测。
ai.method: 设计多专家图像增强模块（MEIEM）融合多种增强策略，提出检测导向回归损失（DGRL）和专家选择模块（ESM），并采用检测导向交叉熵损失（DGCE）训练ESM。
ai.result: 在多个数据集上显著提升低光照目标检测精度。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过联合优化图像增强与检测任务来改善低光照场景下的检测性能。

idea_spark.idea: 可探索将专家选择模块扩展为自适应网络架构搜索，或引入强化学习动态调整增强策略。
idea_spark.risk: 多专家模块可能增加计算开销，需平衡性能与实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将检测结果作为损失函数的导向信号，启发其他任务（如分类、生成）利用下游反馈优化上游模块。

en.tldr: Proposes AMIEOD, a framework that jointly optimizes multi-expert image enhancement and object detection via detection-guided losses, significantly improving detection accuracy in low-light scenes.
en.motivation: Low-illumination images degrade visual perception accuracy; existing methods lack joint optimization of enhancement and detection.
en.method: Designs a Multi-Experts Image Enhancement Module (MEIEM) with diverse strategies, a Detection-Guided Regression Loss (DGRL), and an Expert Selection Module (ESM) trained with Detection-Guided Cross-Entropy (DGCE) loss.
en.result: Substantial improvement in object detection accuracy on multiple low-light datasets.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how joint optimization of image enhancement and detection can boost performance in low-light conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Explore extending expert selection to adaptive architecture search or using reinforcement learning for dynamic strategy adjustment.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-expert module may increase computational cost; balance between performance and real-time requirements needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using detection results as guidance for loss design inspires other tasks to leverage downstream feedback for upstream module optimization.

papers.title: DPM++: Dynamic Masked Metric Learning for Occluded Person Re-identification
papers.summary: Although person re-identification has made impressive progress, occlusion caused by obstacles remains an unsettled issue in real applications. The difficulty lies in the mismatch between incomplete occluded samples and holistic identity representations. Severe occlusion removes discriminative body cues and introduces interference from background clutter and occluders, making global metric learning unreliable. Existing methods mainly rely on extra pre-trained models to estimate visible parts for alignment or construct occluded samples via data augmentation, but still lack a unified framework that learns robust visibility-consistent matching under realistic occlusion patterns. In this paper, we propose DPM++, a Dynamic Masked Metric Learning framework for occluded person re-identification. DPM++ learns an input-adaptive masked metric that dynamically selects reliable identity subspaces for each occluded instance, enabling matching to emphasize visibility-consistent evidence while suppressing unreliable components. Built upon the classifier-prototype space, DPM++ introduces a CLIP-based two-stage supervision scheme, where ID-level semantic priors are learned from the text branch and transferred into the classifier-prototype space for dynamic masked matching. To strengthen the masked metric, we introduce a saliency-guided patch transfer strategy to synthesize controllable and photo-realistic occluded samples during training. Exploiting real scene priors, this strategy exposes the model to realistic partial observations and provides richer supervision than random erasing. In addition, occlusion-aware sample pairing and mask-guided optimization improve the stability and effectiveness of the framework. Experiments on occluded and holistic person re-identification benchmarks show that DPM++ consistently outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods in both holistic and occlusion scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出DPM++框架，通过动态掩码度量学习解决遮挡行人重识别中的可见性不一致匹配问题。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖额外预训练模型或数据增强，缺乏统一框架处理真实遮挡模式下的鲁棒匹配。
ai.method: 基于分类器-原型空间，引入CLIP两阶段监督和显著性引导的补丁转移策略，动态选择可靠身份子空间。
ai.result: 在遮挡和整体行人重识别基准上一致超越先前最先进方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需额外预训练模型、能自适应处理遮挡的度量学习框架，适用于监控等实际场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用CLIP文本分支提供语义先验，引导视觉特征在原型空间中的动态对齐。
idea_spark.risk: CLIP依赖可能引入领域偏差，且合成遮挡样本的真实性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将多模态先验与动态掩码结合，为遮挡鲁棒表示学习提供新范式。

en.tldr: Proposes DPM++, a dynamic masked metric learning framework for occluded person re-identification, achieving visibility-consistent matching.
en.motivation: Existing methods rely on extra pre-trained models or data augmentation, lacking a unified framework for robust matching under realistic occlusion.
en.method: Builds on classifier-prototype space with CLIP-based two-stage supervision and saliency-guided patch transfer to dynamically select reliable identity subspaces.
en.result: Consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods on both occluded and holistic person re-identification benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Offers a unified, adaptive metric learning framework for occlusion handling without extra pre-trained models, applicable to real-world surveillance.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage CLIP text branch to provide semantic priors for dynamic alignment in prototype space.
idea_spark.risk: CLIP dependency may introduce domain bias; realism of synthetic occluded samples needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining multimodal priors with dynamic masking offers a new paradigm for occlusion-robust representation learning.

papers.title: A Novel Byte-Level Flow-to-Image Encoding Method for Network Intrusion Detection Systems
papers.summary: Network-based Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are predominantly trained on tabular flow records, whose one-dimensional representations limit convolutional architectures from exploiting inter-feature spatial correlations. This paper presents a novel byte-level flow-to-image encoding method that converts each network-flow record into a fixed-size RGB image. Continuous features are serialised using IEEE-754 single-precision format and packed sequentially into pixels along an inverted-L shaped trajectory, while discrete features are mapped to byte values and placed contiguously in the middle image row's centre. The encoding is deterministic and reversible, preserving a fixed spatial layout across all samples. Four IDS models are evaluated on NSL-KDD and UNSW-NB15 datasets with both flow and image-based configurations. The image-based representation yields consistent accuracy gains of up to 15.6\% and 12.8\% for binary and multi-classification on UNSW-NB15, and up to 3.5\% and 3.2\% on NSL-KDD, highlighting the potential of byte-level visual encoding to strengthen AI-driven intrusion detection in local computer networks.

ai.tldr: 提出一种字节级流到图像的编码方法，将网络流记录转换为固定大小的RGB图像，在NSL-KDD和UNSW-NB15数据集上提升了入侵检测准确率。
ai.motivation: 传统基于表格流记录的入侵检测系统使用一维表示，限制了卷积架构利用特征间空间相关性。
ai.method: 连续特征采用IEEE-754单精度格式序列化，沿倒L形轨迹打包到像素中；离散特征映射为字节值并放置在中间行中心。编码确定且可逆，保持固定空间布局。
ai.result: 在UNSW-NB15上二分类和多分类准确率分别提升15.6%和12.8%，在NSL-KDD上提升3.5%和3.2%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种将网络流数据转换为图像格式的实用方法，便于直接应用卷积神经网络进行入侵检测。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将不同协议层（如TCP/IP头部）的字节级特征以类似方式编码为多通道图像，以捕获跨层相关性。
idea_spark.risk: 编码的固定空间布局可能无法适应动态变化的特征维度，且对噪声或缺失字节敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受图像分类中空间结构重要性的启发，将一维流数据重塑为二维图像以利用卷积网络的归纳偏置。

en.tldr: Proposes a byte-level flow-to-image encoding method that converts network flow records into fixed-size RGB images, improving intrusion detection accuracy on NSL-KDD and UNSW-NB15 datasets.
en.motivation: Traditional tabular flow records limit convolutional architectures from exploiting inter-feature spatial correlations due to their one-dimensional representation.
en.method: Continuous features are serialized using IEEE-754 single-precision format and packed along an inverted-L shaped trajectory into pixels; discrete features are mapped to byte values and placed contiguously in the center of the middle row. The encoding is deterministic and reversible, maintaining a fixed spatial layout.
en.result: Accuracy gains up to 15.6% and 12.8% for binary and multi-classification on UNSW-NB15, and up to 3.5% and 3.2% on NSL-KDD.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical method to convert network flow data into image format, enabling direct application of convolutional neural networks for intrusion detection.

idea_spark.idea: Explore encoding byte-level features from different protocol layers (e.g., TCP/IP headers) into multi-channel images to capture cross-layer correlations.
idea_spark.risk: The fixed spatial layout may not adapt to dynamically changing feature dimensions and may be sensitive to noise or missing bytes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the importance of spatial structure in image classification, reshaping one-dimensional flow data into two-dimensional images to leverage convolutional network inductive biases.

papers.title: Fusion in Your Way: Aligning Image Fusion with Heterogeneous Demands via Direct Preference Optimization
papers.summary: As a key technique in multi-modal processing, infrared and visible image fusion (IVIF) plays a crucial role in integrating complementary spectral information for visual enhancement and downstream vision tasks. Despite remarkable progress, existing methods struggle to flexibly accommodate heterogeneous demands. Achieving adaptive fusion that aligns with various preferences from both human and machine vision remains an open and challenging problem. To address this challenge, we propose DPOFusion, a direct preference optimization (DPO) framework integrating the property-aligned latent diffusion model (PALDM) and the preference-controllable latent diffusion model (PCLDM), enabling task-guided, preference-adaptive IVIF for both human and machine vision. The PALDM leverages a latent fusion prior and a joint conditional loss to generate diverse candidate fusion results with various properties. PCLDM is subsequently fine-tuned via instance direct preference optimization (IDPO), enabling direct control of the final fusion results with heterogeneous preference signals. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework not only attains precise preference alignment among humans, vision-language models, and task-driven networks, but also sets a new benchmark for adaptive fusion quality and task-oriented transferability.

ai.tldr: 提出DPOFusion框架，通过直接偏好优化对齐红外与可见光图像融合中人类与机器的异构需求。
ai.motivation: 现有融合方法难以灵活适应人类视觉与机器视觉的多样化偏好。
ai.method: 构建属性对齐潜扩散模型（PALDM）生成候选结果，再通过实例直接偏好优化（IDPO）微调偏好可控潜扩散模型（PCLDM）。
ai.result: 在人类、视觉语言模型和任务驱动网络上实现了精确偏好对齐，刷新了自适应融合质量与任务迁移性基准。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用偏好优化实现多模态图像融合的灵活定制。

idea_spark.idea: 将直接偏好优化与潜扩散模型结合，实现任务引导的融合结果可控生成。
idea_spark.risk: 偏好信号的定义和收集可能引入主观偏差，影响泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用人类反馈优化生成模型，为自适应视觉任务提供新思路。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用人类反馈优化生成模型，为自适应视觉任务提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes DPOFusion, a direct preference optimization framework aligning infrared and visible image fusion with heterogeneous human and machine demands.
en.motivation: Existing fusion methods struggle to flexibly accommodate diverse preferences from both human and machine vision.
en.method: Builds a property-aligned latent diffusion model (PALDM) to generate candidate results, then fine-tunes a preference-controllable latent diffusion model (PCLDM) via instance direct preference optimization (IDPO).
en.result: Achieves precise preference alignment among humans, vision-language models, and task-driven networks, setting a new benchmark for adaptive fusion quality and task-oriented transferability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage preference optimization for flexible customization of multi-modal image fusion.

idea_spark.idea: Combines direct preference optimization with latent diffusion models for task-guided controllable fusion generation.
idea_spark.risk: Definition and collection of preference signals may introduce subjective bias, affecting generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using human feedback to optimize generative models offers a new paradigm for adaptive vision tasks.

papers.title: Relit-LiVE: Relight Video by Jointly Learning Environment Video
papers.summary: Recent advances have shown that large-scale video diffusion models can be repurposed as neural renderers by first decomposing videos into intrinsic scene representations and then performing forward rendering under novel illumination. While promising, this paradigm fundamentally relies on accurate intrinsic decomposition, which remains highly unreliable for real-world videos and often leads to distorted appearances, broken materials, and accumulated temporal artifacts during relighting. In this work, we present Relit-LiVE, a novel video relighting framework that produces physically consistent, temporally stable results without requiring prior knowledge of camera pose. Our key insight is to explicitly introduce raw reference images into the rendering process, enabling the model to recover critical scene cues that are inevitably lost or corrupted in intrinsic representations. Furthermore, we propose a novel environment video prediction formulation that simultaneously generates relit videos and per-frame environment maps aligned with each camera viewpoint in a single diffusion process. This joint prediction enforces strong geometric-illumination alignment and naturally supports dynamic lighting and camera motion, significantly improving physical consistency in video relighting while easing the requirement of known per-frame camera pose. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Relit-LiVE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art video relighting and neural rendering methods across synthetic and real-world benchmarks. Beyond relighting, our framework naturally supports a wide range of downstream applications, including scene-level rendering, material editing, object insertion, and streaming video relighting. The Project is available at https://github.com/zhuxing0/Relit-LiVE.

ai.tldr: 提出Relit-LiVE框架，通过联合学习环境视频和原始参考图像，实现无需相机位姿的物理一致、时间稳定的视频重光照。
ai.motivation: 现有基于内在分解的视频重光照方法在真实视频中分解不准确，导致外观失真、材质破碎和时间伪影。
ai.method: 在渲染过程中显式引入原始参考图像以恢复丢失的场景线索，并设计环境视频预测公式，在单一扩散过程中同时生成重光照视频和每帧环境图。
ai.result: 在合成和真实基准上优于现有视频重光照和神经渲染方法，支持动态光照、相机运动及多种下游应用。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需相机位姿先验、物理一致且时间稳定的视频重光照方案，适用于场景渲染、材质编辑、物体插入等任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将环境图预测与重光照任务耦合，利用扩散模型的生成能力同时输出光照和外观，避免传统分解的误差累积。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖大规模视频扩散模型，计算成本较高；环境图预测的准确性可能受限于训练数据多样性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过显式引入原始图像作为线索，弥补内在表示的信息损失，启发其他逆问题中利用原始观测增强生成质量的研究。

idea_spark.idea: 将环境图预测与重光照任务耦合，利用扩散模型的生成能力同时输出光照和外观，避免传统分解的误差累积。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖大规模视频扩散模型，计算成本较高；环境图预测的准确性可能受限于训练数据多样性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过显式引入原始图像作为线索，弥补内在表示的信息损失，启发其他逆问题中利用原始观测增强生成质量的研究。

en.tldr: Proposes Relit-LiVE, a video relighting framework that jointly learns environment video and leverages raw reference images to achieve physically consistent, temporally stable results without camera pose priors.
en.motivation: Existing intrinsic decomposition-based video relighting methods suffer from inaccurate decomposition in real-world videos, leading to distorted appearance, broken materials, and temporal artifacts.
en.method: Explicitly introduces raw reference images into the rendering process to recover lost scene cues, and formulates environment video prediction to jointly generate relit videos and per-frame environment maps in a single diffusion process.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on synthetic and real benchmarks, supports dynamic lighting, camera motion, and various downstream applications.
en.help_to_user: Provides a physically consistent and temporally stable video relighting solution without requiring camera pose priors, applicable to scene rendering, material editing, object insertion, etc.

idea_spark.idea: Couples environment map prediction with relighting in a diffusion model to simultaneously output illumination and appearance, avoiding error accumulation from traditional decomposition.
idea_spark.risk: High computational cost due to large-scale video diffusion models; environment map prediction accuracy may be limited by training data diversity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using raw images as explicit cues to compensate for information loss in intrinsic representations inspires other inverse problems to leverage original observations for enhanced generation quality.

papers.title: ChartZero: Synthetic Priors Enable Zero Shot Chart Data Extraction
papers.summary: Automated data extraction from line charts remains fundamentally bottlenecked by extreme stylistic diversity and a severe scarcity of comprehensively annotated, real-world datasets. Current end-to-end pipelines depend heavily on costly manual annotations, crippling their ability to generalize across arbitrary aesthetics and grid layouts. Furthermore, existing models suffer from two critical failure modes during reconstruction. First, extracting thin, intersecting curves frequently causes structural fragmentation and the erasure of fine visual details, as standard architectures struggle against complex backgrounds. Second, semantic association is notoriously error-prone; current pipelines rely on rigid spatial heuristics that easily break down against the unpredictable legend placements of in-the-wild charts. Finally, measuring true progress is hindered by evaluation protocols that assess isolated sub-tasks rather than holistic, end-to-end data reconstruction. To address these foundational issues, we introduce ChartZero, a parsing framework that leverages synthetic priors to enable robust zero-shot chart data extraction. By training exclusively on a purely synthetic dataset of simple mathematical functions, our model completely bypasses the real-world annotation bottleneck. We overcome curve fragmentation via a novel Global Orthogonal Instance (GOI) loss, and replace brittle spatial rules with an open-vocabulary, Vision-Language Model (VLM)-guided legend matching strategy. Accompanied by a new metric and benchmark specifically designed for full end-to-end reconstruction, our evaluations demonstrate that ChartZero significantly advances generalized plot digitization without requiring real-world supervision. Code and dataset will be released upon acceptance.

ai.tldr: 提出ChartZero框架，仅用合成数据训练，实现零样本图表数据提取，解决曲线断裂和语义关联难题。
ai.motivation: 真实图表标注稀缺且风格多样，现有方法依赖昂贵人工标注，泛化能力差。
ai.method: 纯合成数据集训练；全局正交实例损失（GOI）防止曲线断裂；开放词汇VLM引导图例匹配。
ai.result: 无需真实监督，在端到端重建基准上显著提升图表数字化性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从任意风格折线图中自动提取数值数据，无需预训练或标注。

idea_spark.idea: 利用数学函数生成合成图表，结合GOI损失和VLM匹配，彻底绕过真实数据瓶颈。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实图表分布差异可能导致极端风格下性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 用简单数学函数模拟复杂视觉结构，启发其他领域用合成数据解决标注稀缺问题。

en.tldr: ChartZero uses synthetic priors for zero-shot chart data extraction, overcoming curve fragmentation and legend association issues.
en.motivation: Real-world chart annotation is scarce and diverse; existing pipelines rely on costly manual labels and fail to generalize.
en.method: Train on purely synthetic data; Global Orthogonal Instance (GOI) loss prevents curve fragmentation; open-vocabulary VLM-guided legend matching replaces rigid heuristics.
en.result: Achieves robust zero-shot digitization without real-world supervision, validated by a new end-to-end benchmark.
en.help_to_user: Enables automatic data extraction from arbitrary line charts without any training or annotation effort.

idea_spark.idea: Generate synthetic charts from simple math functions, combined with GOI loss and VLM matching, to bypass real data bottlenecks.
idea_spark.risk: Distribution gap between synthetic and real charts may cause performance drops on extreme styles.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using simple math functions to simulate complex visual structures inspires solving annotation scarcity in other domains.

papers.title: ResiHP: Taming LLM Training Failures with Dynamic Hybrid
papers.summary: Hybrid parallelism underpins large-scale LLM training across tens of thousands of GPUs. At such scale, hardware failures on individual devices lead to performance skew across devices, diminishing overall training efficiency. Existing resilient systems overlook sequence length variability in datasets and device performance skew under hybrid parallelism. As a result, (1) iteration time fluctuations induced by sequence length variability can trigger spurious fail-slow detections, and (2) failures are mitigated through individual adaptations in hybrid parallelism, leading to unnecessary detection overhead and inefficient resilient training. To respond, this paper presents ResiHP, a resilient system that enables robust failure detection and fine-grained adaptation for hybrid parallel training. First, we develop a Detector to accurately identify failures. In particular, it employs a workload-aware execution time predictor that disentangles failures from iteration time fluctuations while remaining lightweight for online detection. Second, we design a Scheduler that dynamically adapts parallelism group sizes, model partitioning, and workload scheduling policies to improve training efficiency under failures. Experiments show that ResiHP improves training throughput by 1.04-4.39$\times$ compared with state-of-the-art resilient training systems under diverse failure scenarios in a 256-GPU cluster.

ai.tldr: 提出ResiHP系统，通过动态混合并行策略应对大规模LLM训练中的硬件故障，提升训练效率。
ai.motivation: 大规模LLM训练中硬件故障导致性能偏差，现有系统无法区分序列长度变化与故障，且混合并行下故障处理效率低。
ai.method: 设计负载感知的执行时间预测器准确检测故障，并开发调度器动态调整并行组大小、模型划分和工作负载调度。
ai.result: 在256-GPU集群上，相比现有系统吞吐量提升1.04-4.39倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在大规模分布式训练中高效检测和应对硬件故障。

idea_spark.idea: 结合序列长度感知的预测器与动态并行组调整，实现细粒度故障容错。
idea_spark.risk: 预测器可能增加在线检测开销，动态调度可能引入额外复杂性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从序列长度变化与故障的区分中，启发更通用的性能异常检测方法。

idea_spark.risk: 预测器可能增加在线检测开销，动态调度可能引入额外复杂性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从序列长度变化与故障的区分中，启发更通用的性能异常检测方法。

en.tldr: Proposes ResiHP, a resilient system that uses dynamic hybrid parallelism to handle hardware failures in large-scale LLM training, improving training efficiency.
en.motivation: Hardware failures in large-scale LLM training cause performance skew; existing systems fail to distinguish sequence length variability from failures and handle failures inefficiently under hybrid parallelism.
en.method: Develops a workload-aware execution time predictor for accurate failure detection and a scheduler that dynamically adjusts parallelism group sizes, model partitioning, and workload scheduling.
en.result: Achieves 1.04-4.39x throughput improvement over state-of-the-art resilient training systems on a 256-GPU cluster.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to efficiently detect and handle hardware failures in large-scale distributed training.

idea_spark.idea: Combining sequence-length-aware prediction with dynamic parallelism group adjustment enables fine-grained fault tolerance.
idea_spark.risk: The predictor may add online overhead; dynamic scheduling could introduce additional complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Distinguishing sequence length variability from failures inspires more general performance anomaly detection methods.

papers.title: Ray-Aware Pointer Memory with Adaptive Updates for Streaming 3D Reconstruction
papers.summary: Dense 3D reconstruction from continuous image streams requires both accurate geometric aggregation and stable long-term memory management. Recent feed-forward reconstruction frameworks integrate observations through persistent memory representations, yet most rely primarily on appearance-based similarity when updating memory. Such appearance-driven integration often leads to redundant accumulation of observations and unstable geometry when viewpoint changes occur. In this work, we propose a ray-aware pointer memory for streaming 3D reconstruction that explicitly models both spatial location and viewing direction within a unified memory representation. Each memory pointer stores its 3D position, associated ray direction, and feature embedding, allowing the system to reason jointly about geometric proximity and viewpoint consistency. Based on this representation, we introduce an adaptive pointer update strategy that replaces traditional fusion-based memory compression with a retain-or-replace mechanism. Instead of averaging nearby observations, the system selectively retains informative pointers while discarding redundant ones, preserving distinctive geometric structures while maintaining bounded memory growth. Furthermore, the joint reasoning over spatial distance and ray-direction discrepancy enables the system to distinguish between local redundancy, novel observations, and potential loop revisits in a unified manner. When loop candidates are detected, pose refinement is triggered to enforce global geometric consistency across the reconstruction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed ray-aware memory design significantly improves long-term reconstruction stability and camera pose accuracy while maintaining efficient streaming inference. Our approach provides a principled framework for scalable and drift-resistant online 3D reconstruction from image streams.

ai.tldr: 提出一种射线感知的指针记忆与自适应更新策略，用于流式三维重建，通过联合建模空间位置和视线方向，实现稳定、无漂移的在线重建。
ai.motivation: 现有流式重建方法主要依赖外观相似性更新记忆，导致冗余累积和视角变化时几何不稳定。
ai.method: 设计射线感知指针记忆，每个指针存储3D位置、射线方向和特征嵌入；采用保留或替换的自适应更新策略，替代传统融合压缩。
ai.result: 显著提升长期重建稳定性和相机位姿精度，同时保持高效流式推理。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过显式建模射线方向和改进记忆更新机制，实现更鲁棒的在线三维重建。

idea_spark.idea: 将射线方向作为记忆指针的固有属性，使系统能区分局部冗余、新观测和回环访问，从而触发位姿优化。
idea_spark.risk: 在极端快速运动或纹理稀疏场景下，射线方向区分可能失效，导致回环检测误报或漏报。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类视觉系统对视线方向敏感性的启发，将方向信息显式编码到记忆结构中，提升几何推理能力。

idea_spark.risk: 在极端快速运动或纹理稀疏场景下，射线方向区分可能失效，导致回环检测误报或漏报。

en.tldr: Proposes a ray-aware pointer memory with adaptive updates for streaming 3D reconstruction, jointly modeling spatial location and viewing direction to achieve stable, drift-free online reconstruction.
en.motivation: Existing streaming reconstruction methods rely primarily on appearance-based similarity for memory updates, leading to redundant accumulation and geometric instability under viewpoint changes.
en.method: Designs ray-aware pointer memory where each pointer stores 3D position, ray direction, and feature embedding; introduces a retain-or-replace adaptive update strategy instead of traditional fusion-based compression.
en.result: Significantly improves long-term reconstruction stability and camera pose accuracy while maintaining efficient streaming inference.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how explicit modeling of ray direction and improved memory update mechanisms enable more robust online 3D reconstruction.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporating ray direction as an inherent attribute of memory pointers allows the system to distinguish local redundancy, novel observations, and loop revisits, triggering pose optimization accordingly.
idea_spark.risk: Under extreme fast motion or texture-poor scenes, ray direction discrimination may fail, leading to false positives or missed loop closures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the human visual system's sensitivity to viewing direction, explicitly encoding directional information into memory structures enhances geometric reasoning.

papers.title: Evolution of Log-Based Detection Rules in Public Repositories
papers.summary: Log-based detection rules remain central to modern security operations, encoding domain expertise that analysts iteratively refine to balance detection coverage against alert volume. Yet while prior work has examined the evolution of network intrusion detection signatures, the longitudinal behavior of log-based detection rules has received little empirical study. We present the first longitudinal analysis of detection rule evolution across two widely used repositories: the community-driven Sigma project and the curated Splunk Security Content (SSC). To compare rule versions based on detection logic rather than surface syntax, we introduce a predicate graph intermediate representation that canonicalizes the logical structure of a rule, together with a tree alignment procedure for analyzing changes across revisions. We apply this method to 6,859 rule histories from Sigma and SSC and find that roughly 56% of rules undergo at least one revision on detection logic. Across rule lifetimes, evolution is predominantly non-monotonic, with over half of rules both adding and removing clauses over time. We further observe recurring reversions, indicating that changes are often revisited rather than strictly accumulated. Combining structural analysis with LLM-based inference and human validation of operational intent shows that roughly a quarter to a third of rules alternate between expanding coverage and reducing false positives, rather than converging toward a stable form. Together, these results reveal that detection rule evolution in public repositories reflects ongoing operational trade-offs rather than steady convergence. Our study raises questions about why rules change the way they do and supports research towards better processes for devising and deploying security rules.

ai.tldr: 首次对公共仓库中基于日志的检测规则进行纵向分析，发现约56%的规则经历了检测逻辑修订，且演化以非单调、反复回退为特征，反映了持续的操作权衡而非稳定收敛。
ai.motivation: 尽管网络入侵检测签名演化已有研究，但基于日志的检测规则（如Sigma和Splunk Security Content）的长期行为缺乏实证分析。
ai.method: 引入谓词图中间表示来规范化规则的逻辑结构，结合树对齐程序分析版本间变化，并利用LLM推断和人工验证操作意图。
ai.result: 约56%的规则至少有一次检测逻辑修订；演化以非单调为主，超过一半的规则同时增加和删除子句；约四分之一到三分之一的规则在扩大覆盖和减少误报之间交替。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全分析师理解检测规则演化的实际模式，为规则设计、部署和优化提供数据驱动的见解。

idea_spark.idea: 利用谓词图表示和树对齐，可开发自动化工具来追踪规则逻辑变化，辅助规则维护。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖公共仓库数据，可能无法完全代表私有或定制规则；LLM推断的准确性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 规则演化中的非单调性和反复回退提示需要更好的规则版本管理和决策支持系统。

idea_spark.inspiration: 规则演化中的非单调性和反复回退提示需要更好的规则版本管理和决策支持系统。

en.tldr: First longitudinal analysis of log-based detection rules in public repositories reveals that ~56% of rules undergo detection logic revisions, with evolution being predominantly non-monotonic and characterized by frequent reversions, reflecting ongoing operational trade-offs rather than stable convergence.
en.motivation: While network intrusion detection signature evolution has been studied, the longitudinal behavior of log-based detection rules (e.g., Sigma and Splunk Security Content) lacks empirical investigation.
en.method: Introduced a predicate graph intermediate representation to canonicalize rule logic, combined with a tree alignment procedure to analyze changes across revisions, and used LLM inference with human validation for operational intent.
en.result: ~56% of rules had at least one detection logic revision; evolution is predominantly non-monotonic, with over half of rules both adding and removing clauses; roughly a quarter to a third of rules alternate between expanding coverage and reducing false positives.
en.help_to_user: Helps security analysts understand actual patterns of detection rule evolution, providing data-driven insights for rule design, deployment, and optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Using predicate graph representation and tree alignment, automated tools can be developed to track rule logic changes and assist rule maintenance.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on public repository data may not fully represent private or custom rules; accuracy of LLM inference requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Non-monotonicity and frequent reversions in rule evolution suggest the need for better rule version management and decision support systems.

papers.title: Distributed Online Learning for Time-Critical Communication in 6G Industrial Subnetworks
papers.summary: 6G industrial in-X subnetworks are expected to support highly time-critical alarm reporting in large-scale environments characterized by mobility, bursty event-driven traffic, and limited radio resources. In such settings, conventional medium access solutions are ill-suited to guarantee reliable delivery of critical traffic, e.g., emergency alarms, within strict deadlines, especially when multiple subnetworks become simultaneously active after a common alarm event, a scenario widely referred as medium access with a shared message. This paper proposes a distributed deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based medium access control protocol for timely alarm transmission in time-critical industrial subnetworks. The proposed method enables each local access point (LAP) to learn, in an online manner, to infer contention conditions from a broadcast contention-signature signal and to autonomously select a transmission pattern over the available channels using a lightweight deep neural network and an (ephsilon)-greedy policy. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently achieves a higher probability of in-time alarm delivery than benchmark random-access schemes, while exhibiting better scalability with increasing network density. For instance, the proposed method improves probability of in-time alarm delivery by at least 7% with a network size of 40 subnetworks, while the gain increases to 21% when the number of subnetworks increases to 60.

ai.tldr: 提出一种分布式深度强化学习MAC协议，用于6G工业子网中时间紧迫的警报传输。
ai.motivation: 6G工业子网需支持大规模、高移动性、突发事件驱动流量下的紧急警报可靠传输，传统MAC方案无法满足严格时限要求。
ai.method: 每个本地接入点在线学习，利用广播的竞争特征信号推断竞争状况，通过轻量级深度神经网络和ε-贪婪策略自主选择传输模式。
ai.result: 相比随机接入方案，所提方法持续提高准时警报传输概率，网络规模40时提升至少7%，60时提升21%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何用分布式在线强化学习解决工业子网中时间关键通信的MAC问题。

idea_spark.idea: 可探索将竞争特征信号设计为多维度信息（如信道质量、优先级），以提升学习效率。
idea_spark.risk: 在线学习可能面临非平稳环境下的收敛问题，以及轻量级网络对复杂竞争模式的表达能力不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物群体中基于简单信号协调行为的启发，将分布式智能与广播信号结合实现高效资源分配。

zh.tldr: 提出一种分布式深度强化学习MAC协议，用于6G工业子网中时间紧迫的警报传输。
zh.motivation: 6G工业子网需支持大规模、高移动性、突发事件驱动流量下的紧急警报可靠传输，传统MAC方案无法满足严格时限要求。
zh.method: 每个本地接入点在线学习，利用广播的竞争特征信号推断竞争状况，通过轻量级深度神经网络和ε-贪婪策略自主选择传输模式。
zh.result: 相比随机接入方案，所提方法持续提高准时警报传输概率，网络规模40时提升至少7%，60时提升21%。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何用分布式在线强化学习解决工业子网中时间关键通信的MAC问题。

idea_spark.idea: 可探索将竞争特征信号设计为多维度信息（如信道质量、优先级），以提升学习效率。
idea_spark.risk: 在线学习可能面临非平稳环境下的收敛问题，以及轻量级网络对复杂竞争模式的表达能力不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物群体中基于简单信号协调行为的启发，将分布式智能与广播信号结合实现高效资源分配。

en.tldr: Proposes a distributed deep reinforcement learning MAC protocol for time-critical alarm transmission in 6G industrial subnetworks.
en.motivation: 6G industrial subnetworks require reliable alarm delivery under mobility, bursty traffic, and limited resources; conventional MAC fails under shared-message scenarios.
en.method: Each local access point learns online to infer contention from a broadcast contention-signature signal and selects transmission patterns via a lightweight DNN and epsilon-greedy policy.
en.result: Achieves consistently higher in-time alarm delivery probability than random access, with at least 7% improvement for 40 subnetworks and 21% for 60.
en.help_to_user: Illustrates how distributed online reinforcement learning can address time-critical MAC in industrial subnetworks.

idea_spark.idea: Explore multi-dimensional contention signatures (e.g., channel quality, priority) to enhance learning efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Online learning may face convergence issues in non-stationary environments; lightweight networks may lack expressiveness for complex contention patterns.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological swarms using simple signals for coordination, combining distributed intelligence with broadcast signals for efficient resource allocation.

keywords_raw: medium access with a shared message

papers.title: SecureMCP: A Policy-Enforced LLM Data Access Framework for AIoT Systems via Model Context Protocol
papers.summary: The deployment of Large Language Model (LLM)-generated SQL queries in Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) systems introduces critical security risks, as prompt injection attacks can manipulate LLMs into producing unauthorized queries that expose sensitive data or execute destructive operations. Existing NL2SQL research focuses on query accuracy, while MCP server implementations provide only SQL-level protections without fine-grained role-based access control. This paper proposes SecureMCP, a policy-enforced LLM data access framework integrating Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with an MCP server to establish multi-layer defense for LLM-generated SQL execution. The framework incorporates five defense modules -- check_policy for table-and-column-level RBAC, explain_gate for cost-explosive query blocking, SQL Interceptor for dangerous pattern detection, Risk Level Filter for SQL risk classification, and DB Isolation for cross-database restriction -- operating in a sequential fail-closed pipeline mapped to six prompt injection types grounded in the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications. We evaluate SecureMCP on the IoT-SQL dataset (11 tables, 173 columns, 239,398 records) using Qwen3-8B. Experiment A demonstrates that defense modules preserve execution accuracy, with EX-in-ALLOW remaining within 65.1%-76.4% across four RBAC roles, matching the unprotected baseline of 63.8%. Experiment B shows that SecureMCP achieves 82.3% Policy Compliance on 2,400 adversarial queries, with genuine defense failures limited to 3.4%. The defense-in-depth analysis reveals check_policy accounts for 78.7% of blocks, while secondary modules contribute an additional 17.5 percentage-point improvement. The Injection Incorporation Rate of 72.5% confirms high LLM susceptibility, establishing the necessity of external policy enforcement.

ai.tldr: 提出SecureMCP框架，通过RBAC与MCP服务器结合，为AIoT系统中LLM生成的SQL查询提供多层安全防护。
ai.motivation: LLM生成的SQL查询易受提示注入攻击，现有方法缺乏细粒度角色访问控制。
ai.method: 集成五个防御模块（check_policy、explain_gate、SQL Interceptor、Risk Level Filter、DB Isolation），形成顺序故障关闭管道。
ai.result: 在IoT-SQL数据集上，防御模块保持执行准确率（EX-in-ALLOW 65.1%-76.4%），对抗查询策略合规率达82.3%，真实防御失败仅3.4%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助AIoT系统开发者理解如何为LLM数据访问实施策略驱动的安全框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将RBAC与MCP结合，实现表级和列级的细粒度访问控制，并针对提示注入类型分类防御。
idea_spark.risk: 防御模块可能增加查询延迟，且对抗样本的持续演化可能绕过部分检测。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴OWASP Top 10分类提示注入类型，为LLM安全提供结构化防御思路。

papers.title: SANEmerg: An Emergent Communication Framework for Semantic-aware Agentic AI Networking
papers.summary: Future networking systems are envisioned to become part of an agentic AI-native ecosystem in which a vast number of heterogeneous and specialized AI agents cooperate seamlessly to fulfill complex user requirements in real time. However, traditional networking paradigms are characterized by a rigid decoupling of communication and computation, which often leads to significant inefficiencies in large-scale agentic AI networking (AgentNet) systems. Emergent communication offers a novel solution by enabling autonomous agents that support task-specific signaling protocols for information exchange and collaborative coordination. In this paper, we consider a multi-agent emergent communication framework, tailored for semantic-aware AgentNet systems in which the user's semantic intent can be automatically detected, inferred, and linked to a set of sub-tasks to be assigned to a set of agents. We investigate how communication and signaling protocols can emerge among collaborative agents with computationally bounded intelligence under stringent bandwidth constraints. Our proposed framework, called SANEmerg, is designed to facilitate the emergence of communication for collaborative task fulfillment while adhering to the physical limits of AgentNet. SANEmerg incorporates a bandwidth-adaptable importance-filter that dynamically prioritizes the transmission of higher-contribution message dimensions, ensuring robust performance in bandwidth-limited environments. Furthermore, SANEmerg integrates a complexity-regularizer grounded in the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle to facilitate the emergence of computationally bounded signaling. Evaluated via an AgentNet prototype and extensive experimentation, SANEmerg demonstrates significant performance improvements over state-of-the-art solutions, achieving superior task accuracy while significantly reducing bandwidth and computational overhead.

ai.tldr: 提出SANEmerg框架，通过涌现通信实现语义感知的智能体网络，在带宽和计算受限下高效协作完成任务。
ai.motivation: 传统网络通信与计算解耦，导致大规模智能体网络效率低下；需要让智能体自主涌现任务相关的通信协议。
ai.method: 设计带宽自适应重要性过滤器动态优先传输高贡献消息维度，并基于最小描述长度原则引入复杂度正则化器，促使智能体产生计算受限的信号。
ai.result: 在智能体网络原型上实验，SANEmerg相比现有方案显著提升任务准确率，同时大幅降低带宽和计算开销。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在资源受限的智能体网络中通过涌现通信实现高效协作，适用于未来AI原生网络设计。

idea_spark.idea: 将用户语义意图自动分解为子任务并分配给智能体，让通信协议随任务动态涌现。
idea_spark.risk: 涌现协议可能缺乏可解释性，且在大规模异构智能体场景下收敛性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物群体中涌现通信启发，将语言演化原理引入网络协议设计。

en.tldr: Proposes SANEmerg, an emergent communication framework for semantic-aware agentic AI networking, enabling efficient collaboration under bandwidth and computation constraints.
en.motivation: Traditional networking decouples communication and computation, causing inefficiencies in large-scale agentic networks; agents need to autonomously emerge task-specific signaling protocols.

idea_spark.risk: Emerged protocols may lack interpretability, and convergence in large-scale heterogeneous agent scenarios needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by emergent communication in biological swarms, applying principles of language evolution to network protocol design.

papers.title: Kirigami-Structured Electronic Capsule for Long-Term Continuous Gastric Monitoring
papers.summary: Ingestible electronic systems enable non-invasive, in situ sensing within the gastrointestinal (GI) tract, yet clinical translation has been limited by uncontrolled transit, short operational lifetimes, and unreliable wireless communication that prevent continuous monitoring. Here, we present a gastric-resident ingestible robotic platform that achieves week-long operation through integration of a bioinspired, electrically triggered release mechanism with a kirigami-enabled electronic architecture. A kirigami-patterned flexible printed circuit board spans the capsule body and deployable superelastic arms, enabling high-density integration of sensing, power management, and wireless modules within a constrained volume while tolerating large mechanical deformation during gastric residence. Stable retention and on-demand disassembly are achieved using thermally responsive polycaprolactone joints that transition from rigid to compliant states under electrical activation, avoiding dependence on variable chemical triggers. Reliable telemetry in the highly attenuating gastric environment is maintained using a dual-band Bluetooth Low Energy and sub-gigahertz module with RSSI- and throughput-aware adaptive transmission, balancing link robustness and energy consumption. We demonstrate long-term, continuous monitoring of gastric radiation exposure, enabling early detection of dose accumulation and providing a promising in vivo alternative to wearable or handheld dosimeters. Swine studies confirm stable gastric residence, sustained real-time telemetry, and safe gastrointestinal passage following triggered disassembly. This work establishes kirigami-enabled integration as a scalable strategy for long-term gastric-resident robotic systems.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于剪纸结构的可吞服电子胶囊，通过电热触发释放机制和柔性电路板实现长达一周的胃内连续监测。
ai.motivation: 现有可吞服电子系统存在通过时间不可控、工作寿命短、无线通信不可靠等问题，无法实现长期连续胃内监测。
ai.method: 采用剪纸图案的柔性印刷电路板集成传感、电源管理和无线模块，利用热响应聚己内酯关节实现稳定驻留与按需分离，并通过双频蓝牙低功耗与亚千兆赫兹自适应传输维持可靠通信。
ai.result: 猪模型实验证实胶囊可稳定驻留胃内一周，实时传输数据，并在触发分离后安全通过胃肠道；成功实现胃内辐射暴露的长期连续监测。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要长期胃内监测的临床应用（如辐射剂量跟踪）提供了一种非侵入性、可吞服的机器人平台方案。

idea_spark.idea: 结合电热触发释放与剪纸结构，实现胶囊的稳定驻留与按需分离，避免依赖化学触发。
idea_spark.risk: 长期胃内驻留可能引起异物反应或堵塞风险，需进一步评估生物相容性与安全性。

en.tldr: A kirigami-structured ingestible electronic capsule achieves week-long continuous gastric monitoring via an electrically triggered release mechanism and flexible circuit board integration.
en.motivation: Existing ingestible systems suffer from uncontrolled transit, short lifetimes, and unreliable wireless communication, preventing long-term continuous gastric monitoring.
en.method: A kirigami-patterned flexible printed circuit board integrates sensing, power management, and wireless modules; thermally responsive polycaprolactone joints enable stable retention and on-demand disassembly; dual-band BLE and sub-GHz adaptive transmission ensures reliable telemetry.

papers.title: XtraMAC: An Efficient MAC Architecture for Mixed-Precision LLM Inference on FPGA
papers.summary: The widespread adoption of mixed-precision quantization in large language models (LLMs) has created demand for hardware that can efficiently perform multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations across mixed datatypes and switch datatypes at runtime. Existing FPGA-based MAC solutions fall short due to limitations in fixed-datatype design, inefficient spatial or temporal resource sharing, and poor support for mixed-precision execution. These limitations collectively lead to under-utilization of DSP resources, limiting achievable parallelism and throughput. In this work, we present XtraMAC, a novel MAC architecture that unifies integer, floating-point, and mixed-precision operations within a single, datatype-adaptive microarchitecture. XtraMAC decomposes all supported MAC formats into a shared integer mantissa product with lightweight sign and exponent handling, enabling dynamic operand packing and efficient DSP resource sharing with constant latency and initiation interval of one across all datatypes. Evaluated on an AMD Xilinx U55c FPGA, XtraMAC achieves 1.4-2.0x higher compute density, reduces per-operation LUT, FF, and DSP consumption by 27-51%, and delivers up to 1.9x greater energy efficiency and 1.2x speedup on representative mixed-precision LLM workloads. The implementation of XtraMAC is open-sourced at https://github.com/Xtra-Computing/XtraMAC.

ai.tldr: 提出一种统一的、数据类型自适应的MAC架构XtraMAC，在FPGA上高效支持混合精度LLM推理。
ai.motivation: 现有FPGA MAC方案因固定数据类型设计、资源共享低效、混合精度支持差，导致DSP利用率低、并行度和吞吐量受限。
ai.method: 将所有MAC格式分解为共享整数尾数乘积与轻量符号指数处理，实现动态操作数打包和高效DSP资源共享，延迟和启动间隔恒为1。
ai.result: 在AMD Xilinx U55c上，计算密度提升1.4-2.0倍，每操作LUT/FF/DSP消耗降低27-51%，能效提升1.9倍，混合精度LLM负载加速1.2倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助FPGA开发者设计高效混合精度加速器，提升LLM推理性能与能效。

idea_spark.idea: 将MAC操作统一为整数尾数乘积加轻量符号指数处理，实现跨数据类型的动态资源复用。
idea_spark.risk: 对非标准或新兴量化格式的扩展性可能有限，需验证对更宽精度范围的支持。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受LLM混合精度量化趋势启发，从硬件架构层面解决数据类型切换开销和资源利用率问题。

en.tldr: Proposes XtraMAC, a unified datatype-adaptive MAC architecture for efficient mixed-precision LLM inference on FPGA.
en.motivation: Existing FPGA MAC solutions suffer from fixed-datatype design, inefficient resource sharing, and poor mixed-precision support, leading to low DSP utilization and throughput.
en.method: Decomposes all MAC formats into a shared integer mantissa product with lightweight sign and exponent handling, enabling dynamic operand packing and efficient DSP sharing with constant latency and initiation interval of one.
en.result: On AMD Xilinx U55c, achieves 1.4-2.0x higher compute density, 27-51% lower per-operation LUT/FF/DSP consumption, up to 1.9x energy efficiency, and 1.2x speedup on mixed-precision LLM workloads.
en.help_to_user: Helps FPGA developers design efficient mixed-precision accelerators for improved LLM inference performance and energy efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Unify MAC operations as integer mantissa product plus lightweight sign/exponent handling to enable dynamic resource reuse across datatypes.
idea_spark.risk: May have limited extensibility to non-standard or emerging quantization formats; needs validation for wider precision ranges.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the trend of mixed-precision quantization in LLMs, addressing datatype switching overhead and resource utilization from the hardware architecture perspective.

papers.title: Self-Correcting Gossip Protocols
papers.summary: We investigate self-correcting gossip protocols with errors. In distributed computing, protocols with errors have been widely investigated in temporal epistemic logics. Instead, we propose a dynamic epistemic logic. We show how to correct transmission errors due to faulty messages without a central authority coordinating protocol execution, how this affects optimality, and how this compares to bounded memory and full information protocols.

ai.tldr: 提出一种动态认知逻辑，用于在分布式系统中无需中央协调即可纠正谣言传播中的传输错误。
ai.motivation: 现有带错误的谣言协议研究多基于时间认知逻辑，缺乏动态逻辑框架，且未解决无中央协调的错误纠正问题。
ai.method: 采用动态认知逻辑建模谣言传播过程，设计自纠正机制处理错误消息，并与有限记忆和全信息协议进行对比分析。
ai.result: 展示了如何在不依赖中央协调的情况下纠正传输错误，并分析了该机制对最优性的影响。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解分布式系统中基于认知逻辑的错误纠正方法及其性能权衡。

idea_spark.idea: 将自纠正机制与有限记忆约束结合，探索在资源受限设备上的实用化方案。
idea_spark.risk: 动态逻辑模型可能因状态空间爆炸而难以扩展到大规模系统。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物群体中错误传播与纠正机制的启发，可进一步研究自适应纠错策略。

en.tldr: Proposes a dynamic epistemic logic to correct transmission errors in gossip protocols without central coordination.
en.motivation: Existing work on gossip protocols with errors relies on temporal epistemic logics and lacks a dynamic framework for decentralized error correction.
en.method: Models gossip propagation using dynamic epistemic logic, designs self-correcting mechanisms for faulty messages, and compares with bounded-memory and full-information protocols.
en.result: Demonstrates how to correct transmission errors without a central authority and analyzes the impact on optimality.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into epistemic logic-based error correction in distributed systems and its performance trade-offs.

idea_spark.idea: Combine self-correction with bounded memory constraints to explore practical implementations on resource-limited devices.
idea_spark.risk: The dynamic logic model may suffer from state-space explosion, limiting scalability to large systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by error propagation and correction mechanisms in biological swarms, further adaptive strategies could be explored.

papers.title: SkillScope: Toward Fine-Grained Least-Privilege Enforcement for Agent Skills
papers.summary: Agent Skills have become a practical way to extend LLM agents by packaging metadata, natural-language instructions, and executable resources into reusable capability bundles. However, this growing Skill ecosystem introduces a new compliance risk: a Skill may perform high-impact actions that exceed the minimum necessary scope of the user's current task, thereby violating least-privilege. Existing skill detection approaches are insufficient for this problem because it is inherently task-conditioned: the same action may be necessary under one user prompt but over-privileged under another. In this paper, we present SkillScope, a framework for fine-grained least-privilege enforcement in Agent Skills. SkillScope adopts a graph-based analysis approach that models instruction-level procedures and code-level operations as fine-grained action nodes. It extracts potential over-privilege candidates, validates them under graph-instantiated user tasks through replay-based analysis, and constrains validated over-privileged actions via control-flow privilege constraining. We evaluate SkillScope through effectiveness experiments and large-scale real-world measurement. SkillScope achieves 94.53% F1 for skill over-privilege detection. In the wild, SkillScope validates 7,039 Skills with over-privileged behaviors, showing that least-privilege violations are prevalent in current Skill ecosystems. In the privilege-constraining evaluation, SkillScope reduces triggered over-privileged action-in-task instances by 88.56% while preserving legitimate task completion.

ai.tldr: 提出SkillScope框架，通过图分析和重放验证实现Agent技能的最小权限细粒度执行。
ai.motivation: 现有Agent技能可能执行超出用户当前任务必要范围的高影响操作，违反最小权限原则，且现有检测方法无法处理任务条件依赖的过权限问题。
ai.method: 采用基于图的分析方法，将指令级过程和代码级操作建模为细粒度动作节点；提取潜在过权限候选，通过重放分析在图实例化的用户任务下验证，并利用控制流权限约束限制已验证的过权限动作。
ai.result: 过权限检测F1达94.53%；在真实环境中验证了7039个存在过权限行为的技能；权限约束减少了88.56%的触发过权限动作实例，同时保持合法任务完成。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户识别和约束Agent技能中的过权限行为，确保技能仅执行当前任务必需的操作，提升安全性和合规性。

idea_spark.idea: 将技能执行过程建模为图，通过重放用户任务来动态判定操作必要性，实现任务感知的权限约束。
idea_spark.risk: 重放分析可能引入性能开销，且对复杂动态任务的覆盖可能不完整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从最小权限原则在操作系统中的应用启发，将其扩展到LLM Agent技能生态，利用图结构表达任务与操作的关系。

idea_spark.risk: 重放分析可能引入性能开销，且对复杂动态任务的覆盖可能不完整。

en.tldr: Proposes SkillScope, a framework for fine-grained least-privilege enforcement in Agent Skills using graph-based analysis and replay-based validation.
en.motivation: Agent Skills may perform high-impact actions exceeding the minimum necessary scope of the user's current task, violating least-privilege; existing detection methods cannot handle task-conditioned over-privilege.
en.method: Models instruction-level procedures and code-level operations as fine-grained action nodes in a graph; extracts potential over-privilege candidates, validates them under graph-instantiated user tasks via replay-based analysis, and constrains validated over-privileged actions via control-flow privilege constraining.
en.result: Achieves 94.53% F1 for over-privilege detection; validates 7,039 Skills with over-privileged behaviors in the wild; reduces triggered over-privileged action-in-task instances by 88.56% while preserving legitimate task completion.
en.help_to_user: Helps users identify and constrain over-privileged actions in Agent Skills, ensuring skills only perform actions necessary for the current task, enhancing security and compliance.

idea_spark.idea: Model skill execution as a graph and replay user tasks to dynamically determine action necessity, enabling task-aware privilege constraints.
idea_spark.risk: Replay analysis may introduce performance overhead and may not fully cover complex dynamic tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the least-privilege principle in operating systems, extended to LLM Agent skill ecosystems, using graph structures to express task-action relationships.

papers.title: LCC-LLM: Leveraging Code-Centric Large Language Models for Malware Attribution
papers.summary: LLMs are increasingly explored for malware analysis; however, current LLM-based malware attribution remains limited by unsupported indicators and insufficient code-level grounding for identifying malicious and vulnerable code segments. To address these limitations, this research introduces LCC-LLM, a code-centric benchmark dataset and evidence-grounded framework for malware attribution and multi-task static malware analysis. The proposed LCCD dataset contains approximately 34K PE samples processed through a large-scale reverse-engineering pipeline and represented using decompiled C code, assembly code, CFG/FCG artifacts, hexadecimal data, PE metadata, suspicious API evidence, and structural features. Beyond dataset construction, LCC-LLM integrates LangGraph-orchestrated static analysis with multi-source cybersecurity knowledge to support evidence-grounded malware reasoning. The framework employs a seven-layer retrieval-augmented generation pipeline, CoVe for IoC validation, and a multi-dimensional quality gate to improve factual reliability and analyst-oriented decision support. Curriculum-ordered instruction data is used to fine-tune DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B and Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B using QLoRA. Evaluation across 43 malware-analysis task types achieves an average semantic similarity of 0.634, with the highest task-level performance in structured report generation, IoC extraction, vulnerability assessment, malware configuration extraction, and malware class detection. In a real-world case study using MalwareBazaar samples, the grounded pipeline achieves a 10/10 structured analysis pass rate, producing CFG/FCG evidence, MITRE ATT&CK mappings, detection guidance, and analyst-ready reports. These results show that code-centric representations, retrieval grounding, and verification-guided reasoning improve the reliability and operational usefulness of LLM-assisted malware attribution.

ai.tldr: 提出LCC-LLM框架，通过代码中心的数据集和证据驱动的推理，提升大语言模型在恶意软件归因中的可靠性和实用性。
ai.motivation: 现有基于LLM的恶意软件归因受限于缺乏代码级证据和不可靠的指标，导致分析结果不可信。
ai.method: 构建LCCD数据集（含反编译C代码、汇编、CFG/FCG等），采用LangGraph编排静态分析、七层RAG流水线、CoVe验证IoC、多维质量门控，并用课程顺序指令微调DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B和Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B。
ai.result: 在43种任务上平均语义相似度0.634，真实案例中结构化分析通过率10/10，生成CFG/FCG证据、MITRE ATT&CK映射和检测报告。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全分析师获得可追溯、证据充分的恶意软件归因结果，提升分析效率和可信度。

idea_spark.idea: 结合反编译代码与图结构（CFG/FCG）作为LLM输入，可能优于纯文本或序列化特征。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量反编译和特征提取，对新型或混淆样本可能失效；微调模型泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将静态分析工具链（如反编译器、图提取）与LLM推理流水线深度集成，形成可解释的自动化分析框架。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量反编译和特征提取，对新型或混淆样本可能失效；微调模型泛化性需进一步验证。

en.tldr: Proposes LCC-LLM, a code-centric benchmark and evidence-grounded framework to improve the reliability and operational usefulness of LLM-assisted malware attribution.
en.motivation: Current LLM-based malware attribution is limited by unsupported indicators and insufficient code-level grounding, leading to unreliable results.
en.method: Constructs the LCCD dataset (decompiled C code, assembly, CFG/FCG, etc.), uses LangGraph orchestrated static analysis, a seven-layer RAG pipeline, CoVe for IoC validation, multi-dimensional quality gates, and fine-tunes DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B and Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B with curriculum-ordered instruction data via QLoRA.
en.result: Achieves average semantic similarity of 0.634 across 43 malware analysis tasks; in a real-world case study, achieves 10/10 structured analysis pass rate, producing CFG/FCG evidence, MITRE ATT&CK mappings, and analyst-ready reports.
en.help_to_user: Helps security analysts obtain traceable, evidence-rich malware attribution results, improving analysis efficiency and trustworthiness.

idea_spark.idea: Using decompiled code combined with graph structures (CFG/FCG) as LLM input may outperform plain text or sequential features.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality decompilation and feature extraction; may fail on novel or obfuscated samples; generalization of fine-tuned models needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Deeply integrate static analysis toolchains (e.g., decompilers, graph extractors) with LLM inference pipelines to form an interpretable automated analysis framework.
